parts.PŘÍSTAVY, PŘÍSTAVIŠTĚ, PŘEKLADIŠTĚ, VÝVA: PORTS, LANDING STAGES, TRANSSHIPMENT SITES, MOORING SITES AND ANCHORAGES
parts.REGULACE TRHU VODNÍ DOPRAVY: REGULATION OF THE WATER TRANSPORT MARKET
parts.VÝKON STÁTNÍ SPRÁVY V PLAVBĚ: EXERCISE OF STATE ADMINISTRATION IN NAVIGATION

p1.heading: Subject Matter
p1.text:
This Act incorporates relevant European Union regulations^1), is based on directly applicable European Union regulations^2), and regulates:

- a) the definition of waterways and their administration,

- b) the conditions for operating vessels on inland waterways,

- c) navigation rules,

- d) the conditions for operating water transport on inland waterways, and

- e) the powers and authority of administrative bodies in the field of navigation.

p2.heading: Basic Concepts
p2.text:
For the purposes of this Act, the following terms shall mean:

- a) „waterway“: a watercourse or other surface water body^3) on which vessels can operate, and „components of a waterway“: waterworks and other structures and facilities listed in Annex No. 1 to this Act,

- b) „navigation“: the movement or mooring of a vessel on a waterway,

- c) „operation of a vessel“: navigation and other activities directly related to navigation, in particular loading and unloading of cargo, embarkation and disembarkation of persons, supply of operating materials, or maintenance of the vessel,

- d) „vessel“: a controllable body designed for movement or mooring on water, especially for the purpose of transporting persons and cargo or carrying machinery and equipment,

- e) „vessel formation“: a combination of several vessels, at least one of which has its own propulsion,

- f) „navigation marking“: signal signs placed on the water surface, banks, and on structures on the waterway, light and sound signals emitted by a designated device located outside the vessel, and the kilometrage of the waterway,

- g) „port“: a set of land, structures, facilities including floating facilities, land communications or their parts, and railways directly territorially and functionally related to and connected with the adjacent part of the waterway (hereinafter referred to as „land part of the port“) and a port basin, water areas necessary for vessel mooring, quay walls with mooring facilities, possibly a sloping bank and mooring dolphins, which allow for vessel mooring, loading and unloading of goods, embarkation and disembarkation of persons, repairs, maintenance, and protection of vessels (hereinafter referred to as „water part of the port“),

- h) „landing stage“: a place designated for the mooring and servicing of vessels for the embarkation and disembarkation of persons, equipped with a fixed or floating landing facility,

- i) „transshipment site“: a place designated for the mooring and servicing of vessels for the loading and unloading of cargo, equipped with a stable or mobile transshipment facility, possibly a facility for short-term storage of cargo,

- j) „mooring site“: a place equipped with a mooring facility designated for the mooring of vessels by means of ropes,

- k) „anchorage“: a place designated for the mooring of vessels by means of anchors,

- l) „employee in water transport“: a crew member of a vessel and any other person who performs dependent work for the water transport operator on a vessel intended for the transport of more than 12 passengers,

- m) „day of rest“: a rest period of 24 consecutive hours, which an employee in water transport may spend at a place of their own choosing.

p3.text:
(1) Waterways are divided into monitored waterways and unmonitored waterways. Monitored waterways must comply with navigation operational conditions. The navigation operational conditions for operating navigation and the method of marking monitored waterways shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

(2) Monitored waterways are classified as transport-significant waterways and special-purpose waterways. The dimensions of transport-significant waterways, including their classification into categories, and the navigation operational conditions for the placement of bridges and other facilities that cross them above the highest navigable water level or below the bed of these waterways, shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation. Special-purpose waterways, the list of which shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation, are waterways on which only recreational navigation and water transport of local significance are operated.

(3) Transport-significant waterways are further classified, in terms of their use for operating water transport, into utilized waterways and utilizable waterways.

(4) Transport-significant waterways are listed in Annex No. 2 to this Act.

p4.text: The Ministry of Transport, in agreement with the central water management authority^4), exercises competence in the area of care for the development of transport-significant waterways and their modernization. In this capacity, it also submits opinions on spatial planning documentation and on spatial measures concerning building moratoriums. These opinions are not administrative decisions.

p5.text:
(1) A waterway is administered by its administrator (hereinafter referred to as the „waterway administrator“).

(2) The waterway administrator is:

- a) the watercourse administrator or the person who performs its administration according to the Water Act, if it is a watercourse,

- b) the owner of the land that forms the bed of another surface water body, if it is a surface water body other than a watercourse, or

- c) the operator of a gravel pit where extraction from water takes place, if it is such a gravel pit.

(3) The administrator of a waterway component is its owner.

(4) The waterway administrator is not liable for damage caused by:

- a) the cessation or restriction of navigation traffic on the waterway due to its modernization or maintenance, or

- b) the condition of the waterway, if they prove that they made every effort that could be required to fulfill the obligation under this Act.

(5) The waterway administrator is obliged to administer monitored waterways in such a way as to ensure safe navigation operations, and to properly mark them with navigation markings and maintain these navigation markings, unless this Act stipulates otherwise.

(6) The scope and content of activities performed in the administration of a monitored waterway and the method of proper marking of a monitored waterway with navigation markings shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p5b.heading: State-funded organization in the field of waterways
p5b.text:
(1) The Ministry of Transport may establish a state-funded organization^22) for the purposes of:

- a) construction, modernization, repairs, maintenance or administration of components of transport-significant waterways, with which the waterway administrator is not authorized to manage property,

- b) construction, modernization, repairs, maintenance or administration of movable or immovable assets used for the purpose of operating a port in the Federal Republic of Germany in the city of Hamburg, which are owned by the Czech Republic or leased by the Czech Republic for a period longer than 10 years,

- c) construction, modernization, repairs, maintenance or operation of ports, landings, mooring places or anchorages owned by the Czech Republic, with which the waterway administrator is not authorized to manage property, or their connection to motorways, roads, national or regional railways on transport-significant waterways,

- d) information support for users of transport-significant waterways.

The Ministry of Transport also decides on changes in the data specified in the founding charter.

(2) The founding charter must contain, in particular:

- a) designation of the founder of the state-funded organization,

- b) name, registered office and identification number (hereinafter referred to as „identification number“) of the state-funded organization; the name must exclude the possibility of confusion with the names of other state-funded organizations,

- c) day, month and year of establishment of the state-funded organization,

- d) determination of the period for which the state-funded organization is established, or an indication that it is established for an indefinite period,

- e) definition of the purpose for which the state-funded organization is established, and the corresponding subject of its main activity; this purpose must not overlap with activities performed by the waterway administrator according to this Act or other legal regulations^23),

- f) definition of the assets that the Ministry of Transport entrusts to the state-funded organization upon its establishment,

- g) designation of the statutory body and definition of the basic organizational structure of the state-funded organization,

- h) date of issue of the founding charter;

if changes occur in the specified data, the Ministry of Transport shall issue an amendment to the founding charter.

(3) The Ministry may decide on the division of a state-funded organization established pursuant to paragraph 1, on its merger or amalgamation with another state-funded organization established pursuant to paragraph 1, or on other changes to such a state-funded organization. Concurrently, it shall issue the founding charter of the newly established state-funded organization or an amendment to the founding charter. A state-funded organization ceases to exist on the date specified in the decision on its division or amalgamation, or on the date specified in the decision on its merger, if another state-funded organization is the legal successor.

(4) The Ministry of Transport may decide on the dissolution of a state-funded organization established pursuant to paragraph 1. Concurrently, it shall decide on the method of settling the rights and obligations performed by the state-funded organization, including the authority to manage state property, and shall cancel the founding charter. If it does not decide, the rights and obligations of the dissolved state-funded organization shall pass to the Ministry of Transport. A state-funded organization ceases to exist on the date specified in the decision on its dissolution.

(5) Activities performed by a state-funded organization pursuant to paragraph 1 letters c) and d) shall be considered as waterway administration for the purposes of financing and pre-financing from the State Fund for Transport Infrastructure.

p6.heading: Types of ports, their use and permits for operating the land part of a port
p6.text:
(1) Ports are divided into public and non-public ports. Every vessel operator is entitled to use a public port, provided that

- a) the port is designed for the vessel by its structural design,

- b) the port's capacity is not exceeded, and

- c) it is not a vessel that clearly does not meet the conditions of technical fitness, or unless a special legal regulation stipulates otherwise.

(2) A port fulfills a protective function if its location on the waterway or structural modifications ensure safe mooring of the vessel and the possibility of safe access to the vessel in case of high water levels, ice formation, or ice run.

(3) The land part of a port may be operated only on the basis of a permit issued by the navigation authority upon the request of a natural or legal person intending to operate the land part of the port. The land part of a port may also include land, structures, and facilities enabling the connection of the waterway to other types of transport infrastructure.

(4) An application for a permit to operate the land part of a port shall contain, in addition to the general requirements for submissions, information on whether the applicant will operate the port as public or non-public. The application shall be accompanied by

- a) documentation containing the identification of the land, structures, facilities, roads or their parts, and railways that form the land part of the port, including a situational sketch prepared according to the cadastral map with their location marked, with the exception of movable facilities,

- b) a situational sketch with the boundaries of the water part of the port marked and the consent of the waterway administrator to this marking,

- c) written consent of the waterway administrator to the operation of the land part of the port, and

- d) proof of ownership or other right of use to the land, structures, or facilities that form part of the land part of the port.

(5) The navigation authority shall permit the operation of the land part of a port if its structural arrangement and the facilities with which it is equipped enable safe port operation and do not endanger navigation safety.

(6) In the permit, the navigation authority shall specify the conditions for operating the land part of the port serving to ensure the safety and fluidity of navigation and to protect the environment. The navigation authority shall further state in the permit

- a) whether the port will be operated as public or non-public, and

- b) whether it is a port with a protective function.

(7) The requirements for the structural arrangement and facilities of the land part of a port shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p6a.heading: Withdrawal of a permit to operate the land part of a port
p6a.text:
(1) The Navigation Authority shall decide to revoke the permit for the operation of the land part of a public port if

- a) the operator of the land part of the public port has requested the cancellation of the permit,

- b) operators of public water transport do not use the port for a long time and its use cannot be expected in the future, and

- c) it is not a port fulfilling a protective function; this shall not apply if the termination of the operation of its land part does not disrupt the capacity of ports necessary for the protection of vessels on the given section of the waterway.

(2) The Navigation Authority, at the request of the operator of the land part of a public port, shall also decide to revoke the permit for its operation if a permit for the operation of the same land part of the public port can be issued to another person who has applied for it. In such a case, the Navigation Authority shall decide on the revocation of the existing permit and the issuance of a new permit in a joint proceeding.

(3) The Navigation Authority shall decide to revoke the permit for the operation of the land part of a non-public port if the operator of the land part of the non-public port

- a) seriously or repeatedly violates the conditions stipulated in the permit,

- b) seriously or repeatedly violates the obligations stipulated by this Act, or

- c) has requested the revocation of the permit.

(4) An application for the revocation of a permit for the operation of the land part of a port must be submitted at least 6 months before the planned day of termination of the operation of the land part of the port, otherwise the Navigation Authority shall reject it. This shall not apply if the applicant proves that they failed to comply with the deadline according to the first sentence due to obstacles that arose independently of their will and which they are unable to overcome by their own efforts.

(5) The Navigation Authority shall immediately publish information on the initiation of proceedings for the revocation of a permit for the operation of the land part of a public port on its official board. Persons interested in the operation of the land part of the public port who apply in writing to the Navigation Authority within 14 days from the date of publication of the information on the initiation of proceedings shall be participants in the proceedings.

p6b.heading: Price for the use of the land part of a public port
p6b.text:
(1) The price for the use of the land part of a public port shall be agreed upon in accordance with price regulations.

(2) The operator of the land part of a public port must not discriminate against individual users when agreeing on the price for its use.

(3) The operator of the land part of a public port is obliged to publish a price list, which contains prices for the use of the land part of the public port, through the river information services system.

(4) No price shall be agreed upon in the case of using the land part of a public port

- a) for the mooring of a vessel and safe access to the vessel during high water levels, ice formation, or ice run, if it is a port with a protective function, or

- b) by the waterway administrator in the performance of activities directly related to waterway administration.

p6c.heading: List of public ports
p6c.text:
(1) A list of public ports is established, the purpose of which is to make an overview of all public ports accessible to the public. The administrator of the list of public ports is the Navigation Authority. The Navigation Authority shall publish the list of public ports through the river information services system.

(2) The Navigation Authority shall enter a public port into the list of public ports within 3 days from the date of legal force of the decision on the permit for the operation of its land part. The Navigation Authority shall delete a public port from the list of public ports within 3 days from the date of legal force of the decision on the cancellation of the permit for the operation of its land part, if the public port is no longer operated by another operator.

(3) The following data shall be entered into the list of public ports:

- a) data on the public port, namely

- 1. the name of the public port,

- 2. the definition of the location of the public port,

- 3. information on whether the public port fulfills a protective function,

- 4. the date of entry,

- 5. the date of deletion, and

- b) data on the operator of the land part of the port, namely the business name, name or names, and surname, registered office address, and identification number of the person, if assigned to the operator.

p7.heading: Rights and obligations of the operator of the land part of the port and the waterway administrator to ensure safe port operation
p7.text:
(1) The operator of the land part of the port is obliged to

- a) operate the land part of the port for the entire period of validity of the permit and maintain it in a condition that enables safe navigation in the port,

- b) publish in an appropriate manner

- 1. rules for mooring vessels at individual berths,

- 2. conditions for the disposal of solid and liquid waste from vessels,

- 3. conditions for water and electricity supply,

- 4. information on the type and scope of services provided,

- c) mark in an appropriate manner

- 1. places for handling flammable or dangerous goods,

- 2. places for the disposal of solid and liquid waste from vessels,

- 3. places for water and electricity supply,

- d) keep records of loaded and unloaded vessels, including the type and quantity of transshipped cargo, and

- e) after consultation with the waterway administrator, mark with navigation signs the places designated for long-term mooring of vessels, for mooring of certain vessels, vessels of certain operators or vessels transporting certain cargo.

(2) If the operator of the land part of the port is not authorized to handle hazardous waste according to the Waste Act, he is obliged to allow a person authorized to handle such waste to collect hazardous waste from vessels.

(3) The operator of the land part of the port and the waterway administrator are obliged to cooperate with each other in ensuring the safe operation of the port.

(4) The operator of the land part of a non-public port

- a) ensures, in agreement with the waterway administrator, the administration of the water part of the port operated by him to the extent of the waterway administrator's duties in the administration of monitored waterways, or

- b) reimburses the waterway administrator for the costs actually incurred in connection with the administration of the water part of the port operated by him.

(5) The operator of the land part of the port is authorized to issue instructions to vessel operators and masters, vessel crew members, passengers and other persons located in the port, aimed at ensuring its safe operation. These persons are obliged to follow the instructions of the operator of the land part of the port, unless the fulfillment of such an instruction would endanger the safety of the vessel or persons on board.

p8.heading: Duties of port users
p8.text:
(1) A person who operates a vessel or a set of vessels in operation on a waterway (hereinafter referred to as „vessel master“) is obliged to ensure that the operation of the vessel in the port does not endanger the safety of persons on the vessel and other participants in navigation, and that no threat or damage to vessels or structures on the waterway, or threat or damage to the environment, occurs, especially during

- a) entry into and departure from the port,

- b) mooring and maneuvering of the vessel in the port and during its handling,

- c) boarding and disembarking from the vessel in the port,

- d) loading onto and unloading from the vessel in the port, or

- e) special events, which include high water level, fire, ice formation, infection or suspicion of its occurrence, injury or death.

(2) The vessel operator is obliged to use only the places designated by the operator of the land part of the port for long-term mooring of the vessel in a public port.

(3) If the vessel master uses a port with a protective function during high water level, ice formation, during ice run or in case of restriction or cessation of navigation, he is obliged to ensure the fulfillment of the duties according to paragraph 1 only if this does not endanger the safety of persons or property.

(4) The vessel master is obliged to report the entry of the vessel into a public port and the departure of the vessel from a public port to the operator of the land part of the port. This does not apply to

- a) vessel masters of the navigation authority, the Police of the Czech Republic, municipal police and the waterway administrator,

- b) vessel masters of an integrated rescue system component sailing to the site of rescue or liquidation operations,

- c) vessel masters transporting persons according to a publicly announced timetable, and

- d) masters of small vessels using a public port for long-term mooring.

(5) In a public port, it is prohibited to

- a) unreasonably use sound and optical signaling and excessively disturb the peace,

- b) unauthorizedly use port facilities,

- c) extract sand, gravel or soil from the waterway, unless it is port maintenance, and

- d) carry out vessel repairs in a manner that impedes navigation in the port.

(6) The method of ensuring safe operation of a vessel in the port and the content and method of reporting the entry of a vessel into a public port and the departure of a vessel from a public port shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p8a.heading: Berths, transshipment points, mooring points and anchorages
p8a.text:
(1) A landing, transhipment point, mooring point or anchorage on a monitored waterway may be operated only on the basis of a permit issued by the navigation authority upon application by a natural or legal person intending to operate the landing, transhipment point, mooring point or anchorage.

(2) A landing, transhipment point or mooring point serves as a protected place if its structural modifications ensure safe mooring of a vessel and the possibility of safe access to the vessel in the event of high water levels, ice formation or ice drift. The navigation authority maintains a list of protected places, which it publishes through the river information services system.

(3) The navigation authority shall permit the operation of a landing, transhipment point, mooring point or anchorage if

- a) the safety of navigation is not endangered thereby, and

- b) the applicant provides the consent of the waterway administrator to the operation of the landing, transhipment point, mooring point or anchorage.

(4) For the permit to operate a landing, transhipment point or mooring point, the applicant shall demonstrate that they have ownership or other right of use to the land and structures necessary for their operation.

(5) The navigation authority shall specify in the permit the conditions for the operation of the landing, transhipment point, mooring point or anchorage serving to ensure the safety and fluidity of navigation and to protect the environment, and shall state whether the landing, transhipment point or mooring point is a protected place.

(6) The navigation authority shall revoke the permit for the operation of a landing, transhipment point, mooring point or anchorage if the conditions for its issuance cease to be met. The navigation authority shall also revoke the permit if the operator of the landing, transhipment point, mooring point or anchorage

- a) seriously or repeatedly violates the conditions stipulated in the permit,

- b) seriously or repeatedly violates the obligations stipulated by this Act, or

- c) has requested the revocation of the permit.

(7) The operator of a landing, transhipment point, mooring point or anchorage is obliged to

- a) mark the landing, transhipment point, mooring point or anchorage with navigation signs, and

- b) provide the navigation authority, upon request, with information on the manner and extent of use of the landing, transhipment point, mooring point or anchorage.

(8) The operator of a landing, transhipment point, mooring point or anchorage is authorised to issue instructions to operators and masters of vessels, crew members of vessels, passengers, and other persons located in the area of the landing, transhipment point, mooring point or anchorage, aimed at ensuring safe operation; these persons are obliged to comply with the orders of the operator of the landing, transhipment point, mooring point or anchorage.

(9) Paragraphs 1 and 3 to 7 shall not apply to the operation of mooring points for small vessels not subject to registration under this Act.

p9.heading: Approval of the technical fitness of a vessel
p9.text:
(1) A vessel subject to registration under this Act may be operated on a waterway if

- a) it is registered in

- 1. the Shipping Register of the Czech Republic (hereinafter referred to as the „Shipping Register“), the Register of Small Vessels, or in a similar register of another Member State of the European Union, another contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area, or the Swiss Confederation, or

- 2. a register similar to the Shipping Register maintained by a state other than those listed in point 1, if it is a foreign vessel whose operator has been issued an operating permit by the navigation authority,

- b) it is

- 1. operated in accordance with the conditions defined in a valid vessel certificate or a similar document issued by another Member State of the European Union, another contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area, or the Swiss Confederation, or

- 2. marked with CE marking or is operated in accordance with the conditions defined in a valid document issued under an international treaty that is part of the legal order, if it is a vessel not registered in the Shipping Register or the Register of Small Vessels, and

- c) it meets the conditions of technical seaworthiness.

(2) A vessel subject to registration under this Act may also be operated on a waterway if its technical seaworthiness for maritime navigation has been approved by the competent authority of the Czech Republic or the competent authority of another state, and if it is registered

- a) in the Maritime Register of the Czech Republic or in a similar register of another Member State of the European Union or another contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area or the Swiss Confederation, or

- b) in the Maritime Register or in a similar register of a state other than that listed in letter a), if it is a foreign vessel whose operator has been issued an operating permit by the navigation authority.

(3) A vessel not subject to registration under this Act may be operated on a waterway if it meets the conditions of technical seaworthiness.

(4) Vessels are divided into

- a) ships,

- b) small vessels, the hull length of which is less than 20 meters; a small vessel is not

- 1. a vessel intended for the transport of more than 12 passengers,

- 2. a vessel intended for towing or pushing vessels other than small vessels, and

- 3. a vessel intended for leading a side-by-side formation of vessels other than small vessels,

- c) floating machines, which are navigable devices equipped with a crane, excavator, pile driver or other mechanical device and intended for work on a waterway or in a port; a floating machine is not a device intended for the transport of persons and cargo,

- d) floating equipment, which are navigable devices with the exception of devices according to letter c); floating equipment is generally not intended for repeated relocation, and

- e) floating objects, which are rafts, structures, objects or solid groupings of objects capable of navigation; a floating object is not an object intended for the transport of passengers and cargo.

(5) Vessel formations are divided into

- a) a towed formation, which is a combination of one or more vessels towed by one or more self-propelled vessels that are part of the formation,

- b) a pushed formation, in which several vessels are rigidly or articulately connected, with at least one of them positioned in front of the self-propelled vessel that provides the movement of the formation, and

- c) a side-by-side formation, in which several vessels are tied side-by-side, with none of them positioned in front of the self-propelled vessel that provides the movement of the formation.

(6) A vessel meets the conditions of technical seaworthiness under this Act if the vessel, its equipment and fittings comply with the technical requirements for the safety of navigation and the protection of human life and health and the environment stipulated by the implementing legal regulation. A more detailed classification of vessel types according to paragraph 4 and their characteristics shall be stipulated by the implementing legal regulation.

p10.text:
(1) All vessels subject to registration under this Act shall be subject to approval of their technical fitness, unless this Act provides otherwise.

(2) The technical fitness of a vessel shall be approved by the navigation authority upon application by the owner or operator of the vessel. The navigation authority shall approve the technical fitness of

- a) a recreational craft or personal watercraft subject to conformity assessment under the Act regulating the conformity assessment of specified products, if they bear the CE marking and their equipment and outfit meet the conditions for technical fitness,

- b) a serially produced small vessel not subject to conformity assessment under the Act regulating the conformity assessment of specified products, if it conforms to an approved type of vessel and its equipment and outfit meet the conditions for technical fitness, or

- c) any other vessel, if it meets the conditions for technical fitness.

(3) The navigation authority shall verify compliance with the conditions for technical fitness of a vessel by means of a technical inspection of the vessel or by checking the type certificate of the vessel issued for a serially produced small vessel. For the verification of the technical fitness of vessels specified by an implementing legal regulation, the navigation authority shall establish an expert commission. The implementing legal regulation shall lay down the organisational arrangements for the technical inspection, the composition of the expert commission, the oath of a member of the expert commission, the procedure of the expert commission and its activities in carrying out the technical inspection of the vessel.

(4) The navigation authority may entrust a legal entity with the performance of technical inspections of vessels specified by an implementing legal regulation, based on its written application, if the legal entity demonstrates that

- a) it owns or has the right to use the equipment necessary for carrying out technical inspections,

- b) the member of the statutory body or the authorised representative, if appointed, has a university degree in a technical field and at least 5 years of experience in inland navigation,

- c) the members of the statutory body and the authorised representative, if appointed, are of good repute under the Trade Licensing Act,

- d) it does not manufacture, import, sell, modify or repair vessels, does not operate water transport, and is not organisationally, personally or financially linked in any way with a person carrying out any of these activities, and

- e) it holds a valid accreditation certificate under the Act regulating technical requirements for products^27) for carrying out technical inspections according to relevant technical standards.

In the decision on authorisation, the navigation authority shall lay down further conditions for carrying out technical inspections. The navigation authority may change the conditions for carrying out technical inspections if the conditions under which the authorisation was granted change. The navigation authority shall withdraw the authorisation if the legal entity repeatedly or seriously breaches the obligations laid down by this Act or the conditions specified in the decision on authorisation, ceases to meet the requirements under the first sentence, or requests the withdrawal of the authorisation. The costs of the technical inspection carried out by the legal entity shall be borne by the applicant.

(5) If the navigation authority approves the technical fitness of a vessel, it shall issue a vessel certificate to the applicant instead of a written decision. The navigation authority shall indicate in the vessel certificate its period of validity for the duration specified for the given type of vessel by the implementing legal regulation. The navigation authority shall set a proportionally shorter period of validity for the vessel certificate if, taking into account the technical condition of the vessel and its degree of wear, it can be reasonably assumed that the vessel will not meet the conditions for technical fitness for the period specified by the implementing legal regulation. The model of the vessel certificate, the data to be entered therein, and its period of validity, which for individual types of vessels shall be at least 4 years and at most 10 years, shall be laid down by the implementing legal regulation.

(6) In cases specified by the implementing legal regulation, the navigation authority shall issue a vessel certificate which, according to the European Union regulation governing technical requirements for inland waterway vessels^5a), is valid in all Member States of the European Union, other contracting states of the Agreement on the European Economic Area, and the Swiss Confederation (hereinafter referred to as the „Union certificate“).

(7) The navigation authority shall indicate in the vessel certificate the waterway navigation zones for which the technical fitness of the vessel has been approved. The waterway navigation zones shall be indicated in the vessel certificate according to the conditions for technical fitness that the vessel meets. The waterway navigation zones for which the technical fitness of the vessel is approved, and the conditions for technical fitness that the vessel must meet for operation in individual navigation zones, shall be laid down by the implementing legal regulation.

(8) The navigation authority shall not issue a Union certificate if another Member State of the European Union, another contracting state of the Agreement on the European Economic Area, or the Swiss Confederation has issued a similar document and this document is valid, or if proceedings for the issuance of such a document are ongoing in that state.

(9) The navigation authority shall approve the type of vessel equipment and outfit according to the European Union regulation governing technical requirements for inland waterway vessels^5a).

(10) The navigation authority shall notify the European Commission (hereinafter referred to as the „Commission“), other Member States of the European Union, other contracting states of the Agreement on the European Economic Area, and the Swiss Confederation of data concerning the approval of the technical fitness of vessels to the extent specified by the European Union regulation governing technical requirements for inland waterway vessels^5a).

(11) The procedure for carrying out the technical inspection and its scope shall be laid down by an implementing legal regulation according to the individual types of vessels or the conditions for technical fitness established for them.

p10a.text:
(1) A technical inspection shall not be carried out when approving the technical fitness of a vessel if the owner or operator of the vessel submits an assessment issued by a classification society recognized under the European Union regulation governing technical requirements for inland waterway vessels^5a), from which it follows that the vessel meets the conditions for technical fitness, and there are no doubts about its technical fitness. If this assessment does not prove the fulfillment of all conditions for technical fitness, the technical inspection of the vessel shall only verify the fulfillment of the conditions not proven by the assessment.

(2) A technical inspection may, when approving the technical fitness of a vessel for which the fulfillment of technical fitness conditions is not proven by a Union certificate, be replaced by a technical inspection carried out by an authority of a Member State of the European Union or another contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area that is competent to issue a document certifying the technical fitness of the vessel; this shall not apply if the period specified by the implementing legal regulation has elapsed since the technical inspection was carried out by another state.

p10b.heading: Extension of validity of vessel certificate
p10b.text:
(1) The Navigation Authority shall, upon request by the owner or operator of a vessel, extend the validity period of the vessel's certificate by a period specified by the implementing legal regulation, which for individual types of vessels shall be at least 4 years and at most 10 years, provided that the vessel meets the conditions for technical fitness. The Navigation Authority shall extend the validity period of the vessel's certificate by a proportionately shorter period than specified by the implementing legal regulation if, considering the technical condition of the vessel and its degree of wear, it can be reasonably assumed that the vessel will not meet the conditions for technical fitness for the period specified by the implementing legal regulation. The Navigation Authority shall extend the validity period of the vessel's certificate even if the validity period of the vessel's certificate has already expired; this shall not apply if it is a Union certificate.

(2) The fulfillment of technical fitness conditions shall be verified by the Navigation Authority through a regular technical inspection, to which § 10 (3) and (4) shall apply mutatis mutandis.

(3) If the vessel does not meet the conditions for technical fitness, the Navigation Authority shall decide on its technical unfitness. An appeal against the decision on the technical unfitness of the vessel shall not have suspensive effect. By the decision on the technical unfitness of the vessel, the vessel's certificate shall lose its validity. The owner or operator is obliged to surrender the invalid vessel's certificate to the Navigation Authority within 10 days.

(4) The Navigation Authority shall also, upon request by the owner or operator of a vessel, extend the validity period of a Union certificate issued by another Member State of the European Union, another contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area, or the Swiss Confederation. Paragraphs 1 to 3 shall apply mutatis mutandis. The Navigation Authority shall notify the competent authority of the other state of the extension of the validity period of this document.

(5) The Navigation Authority may, upon request by the owner or operator of a vessel, extend the validity period of the vessel's certificate by a maximum of 6 months even without carrying out a regular technical inspection, provided that the navigability is adequately ensured and

- a) the vessel cannot be subjected to a regular technical inspection due to a navigation ban under § 22, restriction, suspension, or other regulation of navigation traffic, or low water level,

- b) the operator proves that they intend to carry out an alteration or repair on the vessel affecting the fulfillment of technical fitness conditions within a period of no more than 30 days after the expiry of the vessel's certificate validity, or

- c) the validity period of the vessel's certificate expires during the last 60 days of the navigation season, which means a period of no more than nine consecutive months within twelve months when, considering weather conditions, demand for water transport services, or other external circumstances, navigation is operated.

(6) The procedure for carrying out a regular technical inspection and its scope shall be determined by the implementing legal regulation according to individual types of vessels or the technical fitness conditions established for them.

p10c.heading: Alteration and repair of a vessel
p10c.text:
(1) If a vessel subject to registration under this Act has undergone an alteration or repair affecting the fulfilment of technical fitness conditions relating to the strength of the vessel's structure, its steering or manoeuvrability, or technical fitness conditions relating to special properties stipulated by an implementing legal regulation, this vessel must undergo a technical inspection, to which § 10 (3) and (4) shall apply mutatis mutandis. Until the technical inspection is carried out, the vessel must not be operated on a waterway, with the exception of navigation necessary to subject the vessel to a technical inspection. An application for a technical inspection, to which the vessel's certificate must be attached, shall be submitted by the owner or operator of the vessel to the navigation authority. The navigation authority shall verify the fulfilment of technical fitness conditions through the technical inspection.

(2) If the vessel meets the technical fitness conditions, the navigation authority shall mark the alterations or repairs in the vessel's certificate or issue a new vessel's certificate, if the scope or nature of the changes made so justifies. In the event that the navigation authority issues a new vessel's certificate, it shall mark in it the expiry date of its validity identical to the expiry date of the validity of the previous document. If this document was issued or its validity extended by another Member State of the European Union, another contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area or the Swiss Confederation, the navigation authority shall send it to that state within 30 days of issuing the new vessel's certificate.

(3) If the vessel does not meet the technical fitness conditions, the navigation authority shall decide on its technical unfitness and withdraw the vessel's certificate. An appeal against the decision on the technical unfitness of the vessel shall not have suspensive effect. By the decision on the technical unfitness of the vessel, the vessel's certificate shall cease to be valid.

(4) The procedure for carrying out a technical inspection of a vessel following an alteration or repair of the vessel and its scope shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation according to the individual types of vessels or the technical fitness conditions stipulated for them.

p10d.heading: Register of Union certificates
p10d.text:
(1) The navigation authority shall maintain a register of Union certificates. The following data concerning Union certificates issued or whose validity has been extended by the navigation authority shall be entered in the register:

- a) the business name, name or names, and surname and address of the registered office or residence of the vessel owner,

- b) the name of the vessel, if it is a ship,

- c) the European vessel identification number,

- d) the vessel's registration number in the navigation register, the register of small vessels or a similar register maintained by another state and the state of registration of the vessel, if the vessel is not registered in the navigation register or the register of small vessels,

- e) the type of vessel and the waterway navigation zone indicated in the Union certificate,

- f) the vessel's carrying capacity,

- g) the date of issue, retention, withdrawal and expiry of the Union certificate, or its duplicate, and the registration number of this certificate or its duplicate, and

- h) a record of regular technical inspections and technical inspections following alterations or repairs to the vessel.

(2) The navigation authority shall ensure the transmission of data on applications for approval of the technical fitness of vessels and Union certificates to the European hull database to the extent stipulated by directly applicable European Union legislation.

(3) The navigation authority shall ensure remote and continuous access to the data contained in the register of Union certificates to the competent authorities of other Member States of the European Union, other contracting states to the Agreement on the European Economic Area and the Swiss Confederation.

(4) The navigation authority shall provide, upon request, to a person who demonstrates a legal interest, a copy or extract from the Union certificate or the data contained therein.

(5) If a vessel subject to registration under this Act becomes waste according to the Waste Act, the owner of the vessel is obliged to notify the navigation authority of this fact without undue delay, which shall ensure the deletion of the vessel's data from the navigation register or the register of small vessels and from the European hull database.

p11.heading: Issuance of a duplicate vessel certificate and amendment of data in the vessel certificate
p11.text:
(1) The owner or operator of a vessel is obliged to immediately report the loss, theft, damage or destruction of the vessel's certificate to the navigation authority. Based on a written application from the owner or operator of the vessel, the navigation authority shall issue a duplicate of the lost, stolen, damaged or destroyed vessel's certificate within 20 days from the date of submission of the application. The applicant shall attach the damaged vessel's certificate to the application for a duplicate vessel's certificate in place of a damaged certificate.

(2) Without undue delay after reporting according to paragraph 1, the navigation authority shall issue a provisional vessel's certificate to the owner or operator of the vessel. The provisional vessel's certificate replaces the lost, stolen, damaged or destroyed vessel's certificate and is valid for 30 days from its issuance. Upon issuance of a duplicate vessel's certificate according to paragraph 1, the validity of the provisional vessel's certificate shall cease. The form of the provisional vessel's certificate shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

(3) If there is a change in the data recorded in the vessel's certificate that does not concern the technical fitness of the vessel, the owner or operator of the vessel shall submit an application to the navigation authority for marking the changes in the vessel's certificate within 15 days from the day they learned of the change, unless they submitted this application to the competent authority of another Member State of the European Union, a contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area or the Swiss Confederation. The navigation authority shall mark the changes in the vessel's certificate after verifying their correctness.

(4) The navigation authority shall also, upon request from the owner or operator, mark changes in a Union certificate issued by another Member State of the European Union, another contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area or the Swiss Confederation. The navigation authority shall notify the competent authority of the other state of the marking of changes in this document.

p12.heading: Serially produced small vessel
p12.text:
(1) The navigation authority shall decide on the type approval of a mass-produced small vessel based on the manufacturer's application. The navigation authority shall approve the vessel type if the technical fitness conditions are met. For the approved type, the navigation authority shall issue a certificate of vessel type fitness.

(2) The vessel manufacturer shall confirm the conformity of vessels with the approved type by issuing a type certificate for each vessel manufactured by them for operation on a waterway.

(3) The navigation authority shall carry out random inspections of manufactured vessels put into operation to verify their conformity with the approved vessel type. If the navigation authority finds that a manufactured vessel does not conform to the approved vessel type, it shall prohibit the manufacturer from putting vessels into operation and shall stipulate a deadline by which the identified defects must be remedied.

(4) The particulars of the vessel type certificate and the data recorded therein shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p13.heading: Provisional vessel certificate
p13.text:
(1) The navigation authority may, upon application by the owner or operator, issue a provisional vessel certificate for a vessel subject to registration under this Act, which does not have a valid vessel certificate and for which navigability is reasonably ensured, also

- a) for the purpose of carrying out a voyage necessary to subject the vessel to a technical inspection,

- b) in the event that proceedings for the approval of the vessel's technical fitness or proceedings for the approval of a modification or repair of the vessel are underway and the fulfillment of the technical fitness conditions has already been verified by a technical inspection of the vessel, or

- c) for the purpose of carrying out a voyage necessary to remedy defects of the vessel or to remove it from the waterway, if the vessel does not meet the technical fitness conditions.

(2) A vessel for which a provisional vessel certificate has been issued pursuant to paragraph 1 may be operated on a waterway under the conditions specified therein and for the duration of its validity, even if it does not meet the conditions specified in § 9 para. 1 letter b) point 1 or in § 9 para. 1 letter c).

(3) A provisional vessel certificate issued pursuant to paragraph 1 letter a) or c) shall cease to be valid upon completion of the permitted voyage, but no later than 30 days from its issuance. The period of validity of a provisional vessel certificate issued pursuant to paragraph 1 letter b) is 30 days from its issuance.

p14.heading: Entry of a vessel in the navigation register
p14.text:
(1) The navigation authority shall, upon application by the owner or operator of a vessel whose technical fitness has been approved and whose operator is a natural person with permanent residence or place of business in the territory of the Czech Republic, a legal entity or its organizational unit with its registered office in the territory of the Czech Republic, or another person who is authorized under a special legal regulation to temporarily provide services or exercise the right of establishment in the territory of the Czech Republic^5b), enter the vessel in the navigation register and assign identification marks to the vessel.

(2) The navigation register is a public list in which the following data about the vessel are recorded:

- a) owner,

- b) operator,

- c) identification marks,

- d) name of the vessel,

- e) type of vessel, its area of operation on the waterway and the purpose for which the vessel is intended,

- f) carrying capacity, permitted number of passengers and other basic technical data,

- g) lien on the vessel,

- h) date of entry and deletion from the navigation register.

(3) A vessel is subject to registration in the navigation register if it has

- a) a total weight exceeding 1,000 kg including permitted load,

- b) its own engine with a power greater than 4 kW, or

- c) a total sail area exceeding 12 m^2.

(4) A small vessel, a floating object, and a vessel listed in paragraph 3 are not subject to registration in the navigation register if it is a floating device with a total weight of less than 10,000 kg including permitted load or a floating device whose length or width does not exceed 10 m and height does not exceed 2 m.

(5) The owner of the vessel is obliged to notify the navigation authority of all changes to the data recorded in the navigation register within 10 days from the date of the event giving rise to the change in the recorded data.

(6) The navigation authority shall not enter a vessel in the navigation register if it is registered in the navigation register of a foreign state.

(7) The method of keeping records of vessels in the navigation register shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p14a.heading: Application for entry of a vessel in the navigation register
p14a.text:
(1) An application for entry of a vessel in the navigation register shall, in addition to the general requirements for submissions, contain

- a) the name of the vessel,

- b) information on the type of vessel,

- c) information on the owner of the vessel, if not also the applicant, which includes

- 1. first name(s) and surname, address of residence, date of birth, if a natural person, or

- 2. business name, name or first name(s) and surname, registered office address and identification number, if assigned to the owner, if a legal entity or a self-employed natural person, and

- d) information on the operator of the vessel, if not also the applicant, which includes

- 1. first name(s) and surname, address of residence, date of birth, if a natural person, or

- 2. business name, name or first name(s) and surname, registered office address and identification number, if assigned to the operator, if a legal entity or a self-employed natural person.

(2) The application for entry of a vessel in the navigation register shall be accompanied by

- a) an extract from the commercial or similar register kept in the state of the applicant's registered office or place of business, if the applicant does not have a registered office or place of business in the territory of the Czech Republic; this extract must not be older than 3 months,

- b) a certified copy of the document proving the legal relationship of the owner or operator to the vessel and a document proving the owner's consent to the entry of the vessel in the navigation register, if the owner is not also the operator of the vessel,

- c) a solemn declaration that the vessel is not registered in the register of another state; this does not apply to vessels registered in the European hull database, and

- d) a document on the approval of the vessel's technical fitness.

p15.heading: Deletion from the navigation register
p15.text: The vessel shall be deleted from the navigation register upon the owner's request or upon the owner's notification that the vessel has become waste pursuant to the Waste Act.

p15a.heading: Register of Small Vessels
p15a.text:
(1) The navigation authority shall, upon the request of the owner or operator of a small vessel whose technical fitness has been approved and whose operator is a natural person with permanent residence or place of business in the territory of the Czech Republic, a legal entity or its organizational unit with its registered office in the territory of the Czech Republic, or another person who is authorized under a special legal regulation to temporarily provide services or exercise the right of establishment in the territory of the Czech Republic^5b), register the small vessel in the register of small vessels and assign identification marks to the small vessel.

(2) The register of small vessels is a record of data on small vessels, into which data pursuant to § 14 para. 2 letters a), b), c), e), f) and h) are entered.

(3) A small vessel is subject to registration in the register of small vessels if it meets the conditions for registration in the navigation register pursuant to § 14 para. 3.

(4) The owner of a small vessel is obliged to notify the navigation authority of all changes to the data entered in the register of small vessels within 10 days from the date of the event giving rise to the change in the registered data.

(5) The navigation authority shall provide data from the register of small vessels upon request to

- a) a natural or legal person who demonstrates a legal interest in the provision of the data; the owner or operator of a small vessel does not demonstrate a legal interest if the data concern that vessel, and

- b) a public authority to the extent necessary for the performance of its powers.

(6) § 14a para. 1 letters b) to d) and § 14a para. 2 letters a), b) and d) shall apply mutatis mutandis to the application for registration of a small vessel in the register of small vessels, and § 15 shall apply mutatis mutandis to the deletion of a small vessel from the register of small vessels.

(7) The method of keeping records of small vessels in the register of small vessels shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p16.heading: Obligation to fly the state flag of the Czech Republic
p16.text:
(1) Vessels registered in the navigation register, if they have a crew and are not floating equipment, shall fly the state flag of the Czech Republic when navigating on a waterway.

(2) Vessels shall fly the state flag of the Czech Republic at the stern. No other flag or emblem may be hoisted in the place designated for the state flag of the Czech Republic. When using other flags, these flags must not be larger than the state flag of the Czech Republic, which is hoisted simultaneously with them.

p17.heading: Gauging
p17.text:
(1) Vessels designated by an implementing regulation must undergo gauging before being put into operation. Gauging means determining the vessel's carrying capacity in relation to its draught, marking the gauging scales, and the maximum permissible draught. The gauging of vessels is carried out by the navigation authority for newly manufactured vessels upon the manufacturer's request, and for vessels in operation upon the owner's request.

(2) The document confirming the gauging is a gauging certificate, issued by the navigation authority for a fixed period.

(3) The implementing regulation shall stipulate the conditions for carrying out gauging and the procedure thereof.

p18.heading: Identification Marks and Vessel Documents
p18.text:
(1) The operator of a vessel specified by an implementing legal regulation is obliged to ensure that the vessel is provided with identification marks, load lines, and draught scales. The operator of a vessel subject to registration under this Act is obliged to ensure that the vessel is equipped with vessel documents during operation.

(2) The master of a vessel is obliged to record data about the vessel and persons present on board in the vessel documents; if it is a vessel referred to in § 24a para. 1, one of these documents is a logbook issued by the navigation authority or another state listed in § 24a para. 3 letter b) to e), in which its master is obliged to record data in accordance with the instructions stipulated by the directly applicable European Union regulation governing models in the field of professional qualifications in inland navigation^28). The logbook shall be issued by the navigation authority, according to the model stipulated by this directly applicable European Union regulation, if the vessel operator requests it. The vessel operator is obliged to ensure that only one logbook is kept for the vessel.

(3) The operator or master of a vessel is obliged, upon request by the navigation authority, the Police of the Czech Republic, or the Customs Administration of the Czech Republic, to present the vessel documents to these authorities.

(4) The types of vessels that must be provided with identification marks, load lines, and draught scales, the particulars of identification marks, load lines, and draught scales for individual types of vessels and the method of their placement on the vessel, and the vessel documents with which the vessel must be equipped, and the data to be recorded therein, shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p19.heading: Insurance
p19.text:
(1) A vessel designated by an implementing regulation may be operated on a waterway only on condition of liability insurance for damage caused by the operation of the vessel, arranged by its operator.

(2) The liability insurance for damage caused by the operation of the vessel, arranged by its operator, must remain in effect for the entire period of the vessel's registration in the navigation register or the small vessels register.

(3) The scope and conditions of liability insurance for damage caused by the operation of the vessel according to paragraph 1 shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

Designated Technical Equipment

p20.text:
(1) Pressure, gas, high-voltage electrical, and lifting technical equipment specified by an implementing regulation, which are installed on vessels, are „designated technical equipment“ subject to supervision under this Act.

(2) The navigation authority approves the suitability of designated technical equipment. The basis for approving the suitability of designated technical equipment is its technical inspection and test.

(3) The suitability of designated technical equipment is always assessed when issuing a vessel certificate. Without approved suitability of the designated technical equipment, a vessel certificate cannot be issued for the vessel on which this equipment is installed.

p21.text: Technical inspections and tests of designated technical equipment on vessels may only be carried out by persons professionally qualified and authorized by the navigation authority. The scope of professional qualification and other conditions for obtaining authorization shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p22.heading: Prohibition of navigation
p22.text:
(1) Navigation is prohibited

- a) at a water level at which the safety of navigation is endangered on the relevant section of the waterway; the prohibition of navigation applies to types of vessels or their formations whose safety is endangered by this water level,

- b) upon the declaration of the 2nd and 3rd degree of flood activity, with the exception of vessels of

- 1. the integrated rescue system used in rescue or liquidation operations,

- 2. the waterway administrator in ensuring flood protection and removing their consequences, and

- 3. the Czech Hydrometeorological Institute in providing flood forecasting services according to the Water Act,

- c) on monitored waterways across weirs, their sluices, and in the marked closed water area above and below the weir, and

- d) on a part of a gravel pit where extraction from water is taking place, with the exception of vessels used for extraction.

(2) The prohibition of navigation does not apply to the mooring of a vessel in ports with a protective function and in protected places.

(3) Drifting is prohibited. Vessels sailing downstream with their bow facing upstream and their engines working forward are not considered to be drifting vessels. The prohibition of drifting does not apply to small vessels without their own propulsion not subject to registration in the register of small vessels.

(4) The water level at which the safety of navigation is endangered on the relevant section of the waterway and the types of vessels or their formations whose safety is endangered by this water level shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p22a.heading: Restriction, suspension or other adjustment of navigation traffic
p22a.text:
(1) In the event of disruption to the operability of a waterway or its part due to a natural disaster, accident, modernization or maintenance of the waterway, or other extraordinary event, the navigation authority may, in the interest of ensuring the safety and fluidity of traffic on the waterway, by a measure of a general nature, restrict, suspend or otherwise adjust navigation traffic in a certain section of the waterway for the necessary period.

(2) The navigation authority shall publish the measure of a general nature after its entry into force through the river information services system and notify the waterway administrator thereof.

(3) If the safety of navigation traffic is seriously endangered and it is not possible to proceed otherwise due to the urgency, the draft measure of a general nature shall not be published and no comments or objections shall be submitted thereto. In such a case, the measure of a general nature shall enter into force on the day of posting of the public notice announcing it.

(4) The navigation authority may, with regard to the capacity utilization of a certain section of the waterway or with regard to the intensity of navigation traffic in the built-up area of a municipality, decide on the restriction or other adjustment of navigation traffic in a certain section of the waterway or on a component of the waterway and, in the decision, stipulate measures for the waterway administrator to ensure this restriction or other adjustment of navigation traffic.

p22b.heading: Instructions for ensuring the safety and fluidity of navigation traffic
p22b.text:
(1) The navigation authority may, through an authorized employee, in the event of disruption to the operability of a waterway or its part due to a natural disaster, accident, repair or reconstruction of the waterway, or other extraordinary situation, issue oral or written instructions to a participant in navigation traffic to ensure the safety and fluidity of navigation traffic, while giving increased consideration to the rights and legitimate interests of affected persons; this does not apply if the same purpose can be achieved by imposing an obligation in on-site proceedings. A participant in navigation traffic is obliged to follow the instructions for ensuring the safety and fluidity of navigation traffic. The authorized employee must be visibly identified as an employee of the navigation authority.

(2) If circumstances so require and it is not to the detriment of the rights and legitimate interests of affected persons, instructions for ensuring the safety and fluidity of navigation traffic may also be issued through the river information services system.

p23.text:
(1) Vessel operators are obliged to adapt the operation of the vessel on the waterway to the nature and condition of the waterway.

(2) Vessels subject to registration under this Act and which do not have a valid vessel certificate may only be placed on the waterway for a specified period based on a permit from the navigation authority.

(3) The operator of vessels is responsible for their proper technical condition and safety in operation.

(4) The operator of water transport, the administrator of a transport-significant utilized waterway, and the operator of the land part of a public port are obliged to equip vessels, navigation facilities, or port facilities with radio stations, the number and design of which, depending on the type of vessel and waterway, shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

(5) Radiotelephone operation on waterways shall be carried out in accordance with a special regulation^5c) and in the allocated frequency band for inland navigation, which may only be used for communication between vessels, between a vessel and a shore station, and within the shore service to ensure the safety of navigation traffic.

(6) Requirements for radiotelephone operation and the equipment of vessels, navigation facilities, and ports with radio stations, the method of using allocated frequencies for a specific type of radio operation, and detailed technical requirements for radio stations for radiotelephone operation on waterways shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

(7) The approval of radio stations for operation, including the allocation of frequencies and call signs, requirements for the competence and method of verifying the competence of persons for operating radio stations shall be stipulated by a special regulation.^5c)

p23a.heading: Obligations of the vessel operator and owner
p23a.text:
(1) The vessel operator shall:

- a) equip the vessel with optical and acoustic signalling, rescue equipment, and equipment necessary for the disposal of waste generated during the vessel's operation;

- b) equip the vessel designated by the implementing legal regulation with radiotelephone equipment, which the implementing legal regulation shall specify depending on the type of vessel and type of waterway;

- c) equip the vessel designated by the implementing legal regulation with other equipment serving to ensure navigation safety, which the implementing legal regulation shall specify depending on the type of vessel and type of waterway.

(2) The vessel operator shall equip the vessel in such a way that the state flag of the Czech Republic may be hoisted on it in accordance with this Act.

(3) If it is necessary to ensure navigation safety, the vessel operator or owner shall ensure a watch on a stationary vessel or supervision over a stationary vessel that does not have a vessel master.

(4) The vessel operator or owner shall, before reaching a water level at which navigation is prohibited, place the vessel in a port with a protective function or a protected location, unless the vessel master has already done so. The vessel operator or owner shall, before the winter period, pull a vessel located on a water reservoir ashore above the highest impoundment level or move it to another suitable location allowing for the re-mooring of vessels depending on changes in water level height.

(5) The floating facility operator shall ensure the presence of a person ensuring its operation during its operation, if the navigation authority has stipulated this obligation when issuing the vessel certificate pursuant to § 24 (3).

p24.heading: Vessel Crew
p24.text:
(1) The vessel crew consists of its members at individual levels of vessel operation. A member of the vessel crew at the

- a) management level is the vessel master;

- b) operational level is the helmsman, qualified boatman, and boatman; and

- c) entry level is the deckhand and assistant boatman.

(2) The vessel operator shall ensure the operation of the vessel by a crew in such a number and professional composition as to ensure the safety of the vessel's operation. This does not apply to vessels in a pushed convoy, with the exception of a push tug, and vessels without their own propulsion in a side-by-side convoy, if the vessel ensuring the movement or safe mooring of the convoy has a competent crew in the number and professional composition according to the first sentence.

(3) When issuing a vessel certificate, the navigation authority shall record the minimum number of vessel crew members and their composition in the vessel certificate. In the case of a floating facility, the navigation authority may, when issuing a vessel certificate, taking into account its nature, method of operation, and nature of the waterway, stipulate that a person ensuring its operation must be present during the operation of the floating facility; this person is not a member of the vessel crew.

(4) The number and professional composition of crew members for individual types of vessels, the activities performed by them on the vessel, and the method of determining the master of a vessel convoy shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p24a.heading: Authorization to Perform the Function of a Vessel Crew Member
p24a.text:
(1) Only a person who has been granted authorization to do so may perform the function of a crew member of a vessel, if it is a matter of:

- a) a vessel that is not a recreational craft,

- b) a small craft that is not a recreational craft and whose volume, calculated as the product of length, width, and draught, is at least 100 m^3,

- c) a small craft intended for:

- 1. towing or pushing small crafts whose volume, calculated as the product of length, width, and draught, is at least 100 m^3, or

- 2. guiding a side-by-side formation of small crafts referred to in point 1,

- d) a vessel subject to approval under an international agreement governing the transport of dangerous goods by inland waterways, which is part of the legal system^19), or

- e) a floating machine.

(2) Authorization to perform the function of a crew member of a vessel is full or restricted.

(3) Full authorization to perform the function of a crew member of a vessel allows the performance of this function on a waterway of:

- a) the Czech Republic,

- b) another Member State of the European Union,

- c) a contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area,

- d) a state that is a contracting party to the Revised Convention for Navigation on the Rhine, or

- e) a state whose certificate of qualification for a crew member of a vessel is recognized under a Commission regulation.

(4) Restricted authorization to perform the function of a crew member of a vessel allows the performance of this function only on:

- a) a vessel operated on a waterway of the Czech Republic not connected to the waterways of another Member State of the European Union,

- b) a military vessel^29) operated on a waterway of the Czech Republic or a vessel operated on this waterway by a basic component of the integrated rescue system, municipal police, the navigation authority, or the waterway administrator, and

- c) a ferry boat attached to a guide rope or chain operated on a waterway of the Czech Republic.

(5) In addition to full authorization to perform the function of a vessel master, the performance of the function of a master of a ferry boat operated on a ferry route located on a waterway of the Czech Republic, not exceeding a distance of 1,000 m and not including the area of a lock chamber, is also permitted by full authorization to perform the function of a crew member of a vessel other than the master, supplemented by restricted authorization to perform the function of a vessel master.

(6) Full authorization to perform the function of a crew member of a vessel can be evidenced by a valid:

- a) European Union certificate of qualification for a crew member of a vessel issued for this function by the navigation authority,

- b) European Union certificate of qualification for a crew member of a vessel issued for this function by another Member State of the European Union,

- c) European Union certificate of qualification for a crew member of a vessel issued for this function by a contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area,

- d) certificate of qualification for a crew member of a vessel issued for this function under the Revised Convention for Navigation on the Rhine by a state that is a contracting party thereto, or

- e) certificate of qualification for a crew member of a vessel issued for this function by another state, if this certificate is recognized under a Commission regulation.

(7) Restricted authorization to perform the function of a crew member of a vessel can be evidenced by a valid certificate of competency issued for this function by the navigation authority.

(8) Authorization to perform the function of a crew member of a sea-going merchant vessel registered in a maritime register or a similar register maintained by another state, which is operated on a waterway of the Czech Republic, if it is a function other than that of the vessel's master, can also be evidenced by a valid certificate of competency proving authorization to perform the function of a crew member of such a vessel, which was issued or recognized by the Ministry of Transport, another Member State of the European Union, or a contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area.

(9) Only the holder of an authorization to perform the function of a vessel master under paragraph 1, who has been granted special authorization to do so, may perform the function of a master of a vessel referred to in paragraph 1 that navigates using radar, uses liquefied natural gas as fuel, or ensures the movement of a pushed convoy of vessels with an area of at least 7,000 m^2.

(10) Special authorization for operating a vessel:

- a) navigating using radar or ensuring the movement of a pushed convoy of vessels with an area of at least 7,000 m^2 can be evidenced by an entry in a valid certificate of qualification for a vessel master under paragraph 6 or in a valid certificate of competency for a vessel master under paragraph 7, or

- b) using liquefied natural gas as fuel can be evidenced by a valid certificate of qualification for a liquefied natural gas expert under § 24n para. 4.

(11) Special authorization for operating a sea-going merchant vessel using liquefied natural gas as fuel, registered in a maritime register or a similar register maintained by another state, which is operated on a waterway of the Czech Republic, can also be evidenced by a valid certificate of competency proving authorization to perform the function of a crew member of such a vessel, which was issued or recognized by the Ministry of Transport, another Member State of the European Union, or a contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area.

p24b.heading: Full authorization to perform the function of a crew member of a vessel
p24b.text:
(1) The Navigation Authority shall, upon request, grant full authorization to perform the function of a „boatmaster“ to a natural person who is medically fit, holds a valid certificate of competence for operating radio equipment pursuant to the Act regulating electronic communications^30), has successfully passed the practical skills examination for a crew member at management level, has reached the age of 18, and

- a) has completed training designated for a crew member at management level of at least 3 years, during or after which they gained experience in operating a vessel for at least 360 days,

- b) holds a valid certificate pursuant to § 24a para. 6 proving authorization to perform the function of a „helmsman“, has successfully passed the theoretical knowledge examination for a crew member at management level, and has experience in operating a vessel for at least 180 days,

- c) has successfully passed the theoretical knowledge examination for a crew member at management level and has experience in operating a vessel for at least

- 1. 540 days, or

- 2. 180 days, if they also have experience in operating a sea-going merchant vessel for at least 500 days, or
