- d) has completed training designated for a crew member at management level of at least 18 months, during and after which they gained experience in operating a vessel for at least 180 days each, and prior to commencing the training

- 1. had experience in operating a vessel for at least 5 years,

- 2. had experience in operating a sea-going merchant vessel for at least 500 days, or

- 3. completed secondary education of a technical nature.

(2) The Navigation Authority shall, upon request, grant full authorization to perform the function of a „helmsman“ to a natural person who is medically fit, holds a valid certificate of competence for operating radio equipment pursuant to the Act regulating electronic communications^30), and

- a) has experience in operating a vessel in the function of a „qualified deckhand“ for at least 180 days,

- b) has completed training designated for a crew member at operational level of at least 3 years, during which they gained experience in operating a vessel for at least 360 days, or

- c) has successfully passed the theoretical knowledge examination for a crew member at operational level and has experience in operating a sea-going merchant vessel in the function of a „master“ for at least 500 days.

(3) The Navigation Authority shall, upon request, grant full authorization to perform the function of a „qualified deckhand“ to a natural person who is medically fit, and

- a) has experience in operating a vessel in the function of a „deckhand“ for at least 180 days, or

- b) has completed training designated for a crew member at operational level of at least 3 years, during which they gained experience in operating a vessel for at least 270 days.

(4) The Navigation Authority shall, upon request, grant full authorization to perform the function of a „deckhand“ to a natural person who is medically fit, and

- a) has reached the age of 17 and has completed training designated for a crew member at operational level of at least 2 years, during which they gained experience in operating a vessel for at least 90 days,

- b) has reached the age of 18, has successfully passed the theoretical knowledge examination for a crew member at operational level, and has experience in operating a vessel for at least

- 1. 360 days, or

- 2. 180 days, if they also have experience in operating a sea-going merchant vessel for at least 250 days, or

- c) has completed training designated for a crew member at operational level of at least 9 months, during which they gained experience in operating a vessel for at least 90 days, and prior to commencing the training

- 1. had experience in operating a vessel for at least 5 years,

- 2. had experience in operating a sea-going merchant vessel for at least 500 days, or

- 3. completed secondary education of a technical nature.

(5) The Navigation Authority shall, upon request, grant full authorization to perform the function of a „trainee“ to a natural person who is medically fit, has reached the age of 15, and is participating in training designated for a crew member other than basic safety training.

(6) The Navigation Authority shall, upon request, grant full authorization to perform the function of an „ordinary seaman“ to a natural person who is medically fit, has reached the age of 16, and has completed basic safety training of 8 hours.

(7) The Navigation Authority shall not grant full authorization to perform the function of a „crew member“ if another Member State of the European Union or another contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area has already issued a European Union certificate of qualification for a crew member.

p24c.heading: Restricted authorization to perform the function of a „crew member“
p24c.text:
(1) The Navigation Authority shall, upon request, grant a restricted authorization to perform the function of a "master" to a natural person who is medically fit, has reached the age of 18, has experience in operating a vessel as a helmsman, qualified deckhand or deckhand for at least 100 days, and has successfully passed a practical skills examination and a theoretical knowledge examination for a vessel crew member at the management level. If the subject of the application is the granting of a restricted authorization to perform the function of a "master" of a vessel without its own mechanical propulsion or with its own mechanical propulsion with a power of up to 4 kW, experience in operating a vessel is required for at least 20 days, and successful passing of a theoretical knowledge examination for a vessel crew member at the management level is not required.

(2) The Navigation Authority shall, upon request, grant a restricted authorization to perform the function of a "helmsman" to a natural person who is medically fit, has reached the age of 18, and has experience in operating a vessel as a qualified deckhand or deckhand for at least 100 days.

(3) The Navigation Authority shall, upon request, grant a restricted authorization to perform the function of a "qualified deckhand" to a natural person who is medically fit, has reached the age of 18, and has experience in operating a vessel as a deckhand for at least 80 days.

(4) The Navigation Authority shall, upon request, grant a restricted authorization to perform the function of a "deckhand" to a natural person who is medically fit, has reached the age of 17, has experience in operating a vessel as a cabin boy or assistant deckhand for at least 60 days, and has successfully passed a theoretical knowledge examination for a vessel crew member at the operational level.

(5) The Navigation Authority shall, upon request, grant a restricted authorization to perform the function of a "cabin boy" to a natural person who is medically fit, has reached the age of 15, and is admitted to secondary school education which is completed by a maturita examination or a vocational certificate in a field of study focused on shipping.

(6) The Navigation Authority shall, upon request, grant a restricted authorization to perform the function of an "assistant deckhand" to a natural person who is medically fit and has reached the age of 16.

p24d.heading: Application for the Granting of an Authorization to Perform the Function of a Vessel Crew Member and Procedure After Submission of the Application
p24d.text:
(1) An application for the granting of an authorization to perform the function of a vessel crew member must be accompanied by a medical report documenting the applicant's medical fitness and, if a condition for granting the authorization to perform the function of a vessel crew member is also

- a) holding a valid radio operator's certificate in accordance with the Act regulating electronic communications^30), this certificate,

- b) holding a valid certificate pursuant to § 24a para. 6 documenting the authorization to perform the function of a helmsman and the applicant holds such a certificate, which is a certificate pursuant to § 24a para. 6 letter d) or e), this certificate,

- c) completion of training designated for a vessel crew member at the management or operational level and the applicant has completed this training in another Member State of the European Union or a contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area, a document of its completion,

- d) successful passing of a practical skills examination for a vessel crew member and the applicant has passed this examination using a simulator in another Member State of the European Union or a contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area, a certificate issued by that state on passing the practical skills examination for a vessel crew member, which was conducted using a simulator,

- e) gaining experience in operating a vessel, a seaman's service book,

- f) gaining experience in operating a merchant marine vessel, a seaman's book,

- g) completion of secondary education of a technical nature, a document of its completion, and

- h) the applicant's admission to secondary school education which is completed by a maturita examination or a vocational certificate in a field of study focused on shipping, a document of this fact.

(2) The documents listed in paragraph 1 may be attached to the application in copy; in such a case, the Navigation Authority may request the submission of the original document for inspection.

(3) During the proceedings, the applicant shall submit an identity document to the Navigation Authority, unless a condition for granting the authorization to perform the function of a vessel crew member is the successful passing of a theoretical knowledge or practical skills examination.

(4) If a condition for granting the authorization to perform the function of a vessel crew member is the successful passing of a theoretical knowledge examination, the Navigation Authority shall, after the submission of the application, set a date for its conduct and suspend the proceedings. If a condition for granting the authorization to perform the function of a vessel crew member is the successful passing of a practical skills examination, the Navigation Authority shall, after the submission of the application, set a date for its conduct and suspend the proceedings; this shall not apply if the application was accompanied by a certificate of passing the practical skills examination for a vessel crew member, which was conducted using a simulator, issued by another Member State of the European Union or a contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area. If the theoretical knowledge or practical skills examination has not been successfully passed within 1 year from the date of legal force of the decision by which the proceedings were suspended, the Navigation Authority shall terminate the proceedings.

p24e.heading: Theoretical Knowledge and Practical Skills Examination for a Vessel Crew Member
p24e.text:
(1) The theoretical knowledge examination and the practical skills examination for a vessel crew member shall be conducted by the navigation authority. The navigation authority shall set the examination date so that it can be conducted no later than 3 months from the date of application.

(2) The theoretical knowledge examination and the practical skills examination for a vessel crew member shall be taken at a place designated by the navigation authority before an examination board, which shall consist of at least three members appointed and dismissed by the director of the navigation authority. A person whose participation in the examination would, due to their relationship with the applicant, jeopardize the impartial conduct of the examination, may not be a member of the examination board.

(3) The theoretical knowledge examination for a vessel crew member shall consist of

- a) a computer-based test containing questions randomly selected from a set of examination questions published by the navigation authority on its website, and

- b) an oral examination, if it is a theoretical knowledge examination for a vessel master.

(4) The practical skills examination for a vessel master shall be conducted by verifying the applicant's practical skills in operating a vessel whose technical parameters and equipment allow for such verification.

(5) The applicant shall take the theoretical knowledge examination and the practical skills examination for a vessel crew member after the navigation authority verifies their identity. If the applicant fails the theoretical knowledge or practical skills examination for a vessel crew member, they may repeat it only once in the same proceeding.

(6) The knowledge and skills that are the subject of the theoretical knowledge examination and the practical skills examination for individual functions of a vessel crew member, the procedure for conducting these examinations, the method of their evaluation, the conditions for appointment as an examination commissioner, and the technical parameters and equipment of the vessel that allow for the verification of the practical skills of a vessel master, shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p24f.heading: Experience in operating a vessel
p24f.text:
(1) Experience in operating a vessel, which is a condition for granting authorization to perform the function of a vessel crew member, can only be acquired by operating a vessel specified in § 24a para. 1 and can only be evidenced by an entry in

- a) a service record book, if this entry is confirmed by the navigation authority or another state specified in § 24a para. 3 letter b) to e); in the case of full authorization to perform the function of a vessel crew member, it must be an entry in the service record book related to the certificate according to § 24a para. 6, or

- b) a seaman's book.

(2) The master of a vessel specified in § 24a para. 1 is obliged to record in the vessel crew member's service record book data about this vessel, its owner and master, the function of the vessel crew member, the date of commencement and termination of its performance, and the course and duration of the voyage.

(3) If a vessel crew member specified in § 24a para. 1 requests it, the navigation authority shall confirm the record of experience in operating a vessel in their service record book, unless it has reasonable doubts about its accuracy. The navigation authority shall confirm the record of experience in operating a vessel for a period of no more than 15 months preceding the day on which the crew member made the request. For the purpose of confirming the record of experience in operating a vessel, the vessel crew member shall submit to the navigation authority their service record book and a copy of the logbook. If the navigation authority has reasonable doubts about the accuracy of the record of experience in the service record book or the copy of the logbook, it may request the submission of the original logbook or other document containing data on experience in operating a vessel for inspection.

p24g.heading: Issuance of the European Union certificate of qualification for a vessel crew member and the certificate of competency for a vessel crew member
p24g.text:
(1) If the navigation authority grants an application for full authorization to perform the function of a vessel crew member, it shall issue, instead of a written decision, a European Union certificate of qualification for a vessel crew member in accordance with the model stipulated by directly applicable European Union regulation governing models in the field of professional qualifications in inland navigation^28). In the case of a European Union certificate of qualification for a vessel master, the navigation authority shall issue it together with a service record book. In the case of a European Union certificate of qualification for a vessel crew member other than a master, this certificate shall form a single document with the service record book.

(2) If the navigation authority grants an application for limited authorization to perform the function of a vessel crew member, it shall issue, instead of a written decision, a certificate of competency for a vessel crew member; in the case of a certificate of competency for a vessel master, it shall indicate the scope of the granted authorization therein. The certificate of competency for a vessel crew member shall form a single document with the service record book.

(3) The model of the certificate of competency for a vessel crew member and the service record book shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p24h.heading: Special authorization to perform the function of a vessel master
p24h.text:
(1) Upon request, the Navigation Authority shall grant a special authorisation to perform the function of a master of a vessel navigating by radar to a natural person who has successfully passed an examination of practical skills necessary for navigating a vessel by radar and

- a) holds a valid certificate pursuant to § 24a (6) proving authorisation to perform the function of a master of a vessel or meets the conditions for granting full authorisation to perform the function of a master of a vessel, if applying simultaneously for the granting of such authorisation, or

- b) holds a valid master's certificate of competency or meets the conditions for granting restricted authorisation to perform the function of a master of a vessel, if applying simultaneously for the granting of such authorisation.

(2) Upon request, the Navigation Authority shall grant a special authorisation to perform the function of a master of a vessel that ensures the movement of a pushed convoy of vessels with an area of at least 7,000 m^2, to a natural person who has at least 720 days of experience in operating a vessel, of which at least 540 days constitute experience in the function of a master of a vessel specified in § 24a (1) and 180 days constitute experience in the function of a helmsman on a vessel that ensures the movement of such a convoy of vessels, and

- a) holds a valid certificate pursuant to § 24a (6) proving authorisation to perform the function of a master of a vessel or meets the conditions for granting full authorisation to perform the function of a master of a vessel, if applying simultaneously for the granting of such authorisation, or

- b) holds a valid master's certificate of competency or meets the conditions for granting restricted authorisation to perform the function of a master of a vessel, if applying simultaneously for the granting of such authorisation.

(3) Experience in operating a vessel can only be proven by an entry in the service record book confirmed by the Navigation Authority or another state specified in § 24a (3) (b) to (e).

(4) The application must be accompanied by

- a) a valid certificate pursuant to § 24a (6) (d) or (e), if the applicant is its holder,

- b) a certificate of passing an examination of practical skills necessary for navigating a vessel by radar, which was conducted using a simulator, issued by another Member State of the European Union or a contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area, if the applicant for the granting of a special authorisation to perform the function of a master of a vessel navigating by radar is its holder, and

- c) a service record book, if the subject of the application is the granting of a special authorisation to perform the function of a master of a vessel that ensures the movement of a pushed convoy of vessels with an area of at least 7,000 m^2.

(5) The documents listed in paragraph 4 may be attached to the application in copy; in such a case, the Navigation Authority may request the submission of the original document for inspection.

(6) During the proceedings for granting a special authorisation, the applicant shall submit an identity document to the Navigation Authority, if the subject of the application is the granting of a special authorisation to perform the function of

- a) a master of a vessel navigating by radar and the applicant has attached to the application a certificate of passing an examination of practical skills necessary for navigating a vessel by radar, which was conducted using a simulator, issued by another Member State of the European Union or a contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area, or

- b) a master of a vessel that ensures the movement of a pushed convoy of vessels with an area of at least 7,000 m^2.

(7) After submitting an application for the granting of a special authorisation to perform the function of a master of a vessel navigating by radar, the Navigation Authority shall set a date for conducting the examination of practical skills necessary for navigating a vessel by radar and suspend the proceedings; this shall not apply if the application was accompanied by a certificate of passing an examination of practical skills necessary for navigating a vessel by radar, which was conducted using a simulator, issued by another Member State of the European Union or a contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area. If the examination of practical skills is not successfully passed within 1 year from the date of entry into force of the resolution by which the proceedings were suspended, the Navigation Authority shall terminate the proceedings.

(8) For the conduct of the examination of practical skills necessary for navigating a vessel by radar, its taking, the composition of the examination board, the verification of the applicant's identity, and the repetition of the examination, § 24e (1), (2), (4) and (5) shall apply mutatis mutandis.

(9) The skills that are the subject of the examination of practical skills necessary for navigating a vessel by radar, the course of conducting this examination, the method of its evaluation, the conditions for appointment as an examination commissioner, and the technical parameters and equipment of the vessel that enable the verification of practical skills, shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p24i.heading: Granting of special authorisation to perform the function of a master of a vessel
p24i.text:
(1) If the Navigation Authority grants an application for a special authorisation to perform the function of a master of a vessel navigating by radar or a vessel that ensures the movement of a pushed convoy of vessels with an area of at least 7,000 m^2, submitted by an applicant meeting the condition pursuant to § 24h (1) (a) or § 24h (2) (a), it shall issue a European Union certificate of qualification for a master of a vessel instead of a written decision, in which it shall indicate the granting thereof. This shall not apply if the applicant for the special authorisation holds a valid certificate pursuant to § 24a (6) issued by another state; in such a case, the Navigation Authority shall inform that state of the granting of the special authorisation to the applicant.

(2) If the Navigation Authority grants an application for a special authorisation to perform the function of a master of a vessel navigating by radar or a vessel that ensures the movement of a pushed convoy of vessels with an area of at least 7,000 m^2, submitted by an applicant meeting the condition pursuant to § 24h (1) (b) or § 24h (2) (b), it shall indicate the granting thereof in the master's certificate of competency.

(3) The holder of a European Union certificate of qualification for a master of a vessel is obliged to surrender the original certificate to the Navigation Authority within 15 days from the date of issue of the new certificate pursuant to paragraph 1. The holder of a master's certificate of competency is obliged to submit this certificate to the Navigation Authority for the purpose of indicating the data pursuant to paragraph 2.

p24j.heading: Validity of the European Union certificate of qualification of a vessel crew member and the certificate of competence of a vessel crew member and the procedure for the expiry of their validity
p24j.text:
(1) The European Union certificate of qualification of a vessel crew member and the certificate of competence of a vessel crew member shall be valid

- a) for a period of 13 years, but no later than the day preceding the day on which its holder reaches the age of 60, in the case of a European Union certificate of qualification of a vessel master or a certificate of competence of a vessel master,

- b) until the day preceding the day on which its holder reaches the age of 60, in the case of a European Union certificate of qualification of a vessel crew member other than a master or a certificate of competence of a vessel crew member other than a master,

- c) for a period of 5 years, but no later than the day preceding the day on which its holder reaches the age of 70, if they certify the authorisation to perform the function of a vessel crew member for the period after reaching the age of 60, or

- d) for a period of 2 years, if they certify the authorisation to perform the function of a vessel crew member for the period after reaching the age of 70.

(2) The Navigation Authority shall, upon request submitted due to the expiry of the validity period of a European Union certificate of qualification of a vessel master pursuant to § 24a para. 6 letter a) to c), issue a new European Union certificate of qualification of a vessel master instead of a written decision, if the applicant is medically fit. In the new certificate, the Navigation Authority shall indicate the special authorisation to perform the function of a vessel master that was indicated in the original certificate. The holder is obliged to hand over the original certificate to the Navigation Authority within 15 days from the date of issue of the new certificate.

(3) The Navigation Authority shall, upon request submitted due to the expiry of the validity period of a European Union certificate pursuant to § 24a para. 6 letter a) to c), which certifies the authorisation to perform the function of a vessel crew member other than a master, indicate its new validity period in this certificate, if the applicant is medically fit. The holder is obliged to submit the certificate to the Navigation Authority for this purpose.

(4) The Navigation Authority shall, upon request submitted due to the expiry of the validity period of a certificate of competence of a vessel crew member, indicate its new validity period in this certificate, if the applicant is medically fit. The holder is obliged to submit the certificate to the Navigation Authority for this purpose.

(5) A new medical report certifying the applicant's medical fitness must be attached to the application pursuant to paragraph 2, 3 or 4. The new medical report may be attached to the application in copy; in such a case, the Navigation Authority may request the submission of the original report for inspection. The applicant shall submit an identity document to the Navigation Authority during the proceedings. If the Navigation Authority grants the application, it shall not issue a written decision.

p24k.heading: Medical fitness of a vessel crew member
p24k.text:
(1) The „medical fitness“ of a vessel crew member means their physical and mental capacity to perform the duties of a vessel crew member.

(2) If the medical report indicates that an applicant for authorization to perform the duties of a vessel crew member is medically fit with a condition, the navigation authority shall condition the authorization to perform the duties of a vessel crew member and record this condition in the relevant document. If the medical report, with regard to the ascertained health status of the applicant for authorization to perform the duties of a vessel crew member, was issued for a limited period, the navigation authority shall indicate the period of validity in the European Union certificate of qualification for vessel crew members or the certificate of competency for vessel crew members so that it corresponds to this limited period; this shall not apply if, according to § 24j (1), the period of validity of the certificate or competency card would be shorter.

(3) If the operator or master of a vessel, or the navigation authority, has reasonable suspicion that the holder of a European Union certificate of qualification for vessel crew members issued by the navigation authority or a certificate of competency for vessel crew members has experienced a change in health status that could lead to the loss or conditioning of their medical fitness, they shall send a written request, stating the reasons for their suspicion, for the holder to undergo a medical examination. If the request was made by the operator or master of the vessel, they shall also send it to the navigation authority without undue delay. The holder of a European Union certificate of qualification for vessel crew members or a certificate of competency for vessel crew members is obliged, within 1 month from the date of delivery of the request, to

- a) undergo a medical examination and

- b) inform the navigation authority of the assessing physician with whom they underwent the medical examination.

(4) In the event of reasonable suspicion that a change in the health status of the holder of a certificate according to § 24a (6) or a certificate of competency for vessel crew members may seriously endanger navigation safety, the navigation authority shall prohibit them from performing the duties of a vessel crew member. An appeal against this decision shall not have suspensive effect. If the reasons for which this decision was issued cease to exist, the navigation authority shall revoke the decision.

(5) The assessing physician shall send the medical report issued on the basis of the medical examination without undue delay to the assessed person, to the vessel operator on whose vessel the assessed person performs the duties of a vessel crew member, if known to the physician, and to the navigation authority.

(6) If the medical report issued on the basis of the medical examination, which the holder of a European Union certificate of qualification for vessel crew members or a certificate of competency for vessel crew members underwent based on a request according to paragraph 3, indicates that they are medically fit with a condition that is not yet recorded in the relevant document, the navigation authority shall condition the authorization to perform the duties of a vessel crew member and record this condition in the relevant document. If the medical report, with regard to the ascertained health status of the holder of a European Union certificate of qualification for vessel crew members or a certificate of competency for vessel crew members, was issued for a limited period, the navigation authority shall indicate the period of their new validity in the certificate or competency card so that it corresponds to this limited period; this shall not apply if, according to § 24j (1), the period of validity of the certificate or competency card would be shorter. In these cases, the holder of a European Union certificate of qualification for vessel crew members or a certificate of competency for vessel crew members is obliged to submit this document to the navigation authority within 10 days from the date of receipt of the medical report.

(7) If the holder of a European Union certificate of qualification for vessel crew members or a certificate of competency for vessel crew members fails to inform the navigation authority within the stipulated period of the assessing physician with whom they underwent the medical examination based on a request according to paragraph 3, the navigation authority shall request them to provide this information within 15 days from the date of delivery of the repeated request, with instruction on the consequences in case of non-compliance.

(8) If the holder of a European Union certificate of qualification for vessel crew members or a certificate of competency for vessel crew members fails to inform, even within the period stipulated in the repeated request, of the assessing physician with whom they underwent the medical examination, or if the navigation authority ascertains that they did not undergo the medical examination with the physician specified in the notification, or if the medical report issued on the basis of the medical examination indicates that they are medically unfit to perform the duties of a vessel crew member, the navigation authority shall revoke their authorization to perform the duties of a vessel crew member and, if a decision was made to prohibit activities consisting of performing the duties of a vessel crew member, this decision shall be cancelled. If the navigation authority has decided to revoke the authorization to perform the duties of a vessel crew member, the holder of a European Union certificate of qualification for vessel crew members or a certificate of competency for vessel crew members is obliged to surrender this document to the navigation authority within 15 days from the date the decision becomes final.

(9) Diseases, defects, and conditions that exclude or condition the medical fitness of a vessel crew member or are grounds for issuing a medical report for a limited period, the types of examinations and their content, and the requirements for a medical report shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p24l.heading: Loss of knowledge or skills necessary to perform the duties of a vessel crew member
p24l.text:
(1) If the navigation authority has reasonable suspicion that the holder of a European Union certificate of qualification for a crew member of a vessel issued by the navigation authority or a crew member's certificate of competency has lost the knowledge or skills necessary to perform the function of a crew member of a vessel or to operate a vessel navigating by radar or a vessel that ensures the movement of a pushed convoy of vessels with an area of at least 7,000 m^2, it shall invite them in writing, stating the reasons for its suspicion, to undergo a re-examination of theoretical knowledge or practical skills by a specified deadline, and shall instruct them in the invitation about the consequences of not undergoing the re-examination. The holder of a European Union certificate of qualification for a crew member of a vessel or a crew member's certificate of competency is obliged to undergo a re-examination of theoretical knowledge or practical skills by the specified deadline.

(2) For the conduct of the re-examination of theoretical knowledge or practical skills, the place and form of the re-examination, the composition of the examination board, the verification of the identity of the person undergoing the re-examination, and the repetition of the examination, § 24e (1) and (2), § 24e (3) a), and § 24e (4) and (5) shall apply mutatis mutandis.

(3) In the event of reasonable suspicion that the loss of knowledge or skills necessary to perform the function of a crew member of a vessel or to operate a vessel navigating by radar or a vessel that ensures the movement of a pushed convoy of vessels with an area of at least 7,000 m^2 by the holder of a certificate pursuant to § 24a (6) or a crew member's certificate of competency may seriously endanger navigation safety, the navigation authority shall prohibit them from engaging in activities consisting of performing the function of a crew member of a vessel or operating a vessel navigating by radar or a vessel that ensures the movement of a pushed convoy of vessels with an area of at least 7,000 m^2. An appeal against this decision shall not have suspensive effect. If the reasons for which this decision was issued cease to exist, the navigation authority shall revoke the decision.

(4) If the holder of a European Union certificate of qualification for a crew member of a vessel or a crew member's certificate of competency does not undergo the re-examination by the specified deadline or fails this re-examination, the navigation authority shall withdraw their authorization to perform the function of a crew member of a vessel or the special authorization to perform the function of a vessel master navigating by radar or a vessel that ensures the movement of a pushed convoy of vessels with an area of at least 7,000 m^2.

(5) If the navigation authority has decided to withdraw the authorization to perform the function of a crew member of a vessel, the holder of a European Union certificate of qualification for a crew member of a vessel or a crew member's certificate of competency is obliged to hand over this document to the navigation authority within 15 days from the date the decision becomes final. If the navigation authority has decided to withdraw the special authorization to perform the function of a vessel master navigating by radar or a vessel that ensures the movement of a pushed convoy of vessels with an area of at least 7,000 m^2, the holder of

- a) a European Union certificate of qualification for a crew member of a vessel is obliged to hand over this document to the navigation authority within 15 days from the date the decision becomes final; the navigation authority shall issue a new certificate in which the special authorization that was withdrawn will not be indicated, or

- b) a crew member's certificate of competency is obliged to submit this document to the navigation authority within 15 days from the date the decision becomes final; the navigation authority shall indicate the withdrawal of this special authorization in the certificate.

(6) The knowledge and skills that are the subject of the re-examination of theoretical knowledge and practical skills for individual functions of a crew member of a vessel or a vessel master navigating by radar or a vessel that ensures the movement of a pushed convoy of vessels with an area of at least 7,000 m^2, the course of the re-examination, the method of its evaluation, the conditions for appointment as an examination commissioner, and the technical parameters and equipment of the vessel that enable the re-examination of the practical skills of the vessel master shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p24m.heading: Loss, destruction, theft or damage of a European Union certificate of qualification for a crew member of a vessel, a crew member's certificate of competency or a service record book, or a change in the data contained therein
p24m.text:
(1) The holder of a European Union certificate of qualification for a crew member of a vessel or a crew member's certificate of competency is obliged to report the loss, destruction, theft or damage of the certificate or competency card, or a change in their first name(s) or surname to the navigation authority within 1 month from the date on which this fact occurred; in the case of theft of the certificate or competency card or a change in the first name(s) or surname, they shall simultaneously submit documents proving this fact. If the notifier so requests, the navigation authority shall issue a new certificate or a new competency card with a validity period corresponding to the validity period of the original document. If the original certificate or competency card has been damaged or the first name(s) or surname of its holder has changed, the notifier is obliged to hand over the original document to the navigation authority within 15 days from the date of

- a) issuance of the new certificate or competency card, or

- b) reporting of this fact, if they do not request the issuance of a new certificate or competency card.

(2) For the procedure in case of loss, destruction, theft or damage of a crew member's service record book or a change in their first name(s) or surname, paragraph 1 shall apply mutatis mutandis.

p24n.heading: Experts on board the vessel
p24n.text:
(1) Activities on a vessel intended for the carriage of more than 12 passengers, consisting of providing assistance to passengers in protecting their rights and to persons with disabilities or reduced mobility, and activities related to the protection of passengers in the event of an accident, boarding or sinking of the vessel, or other imminent danger (hereinafter referred to as "passenger transport expert activity") may only be performed by a person who has been granted authorization to do so.

(2) Activities on a vessel specified in § 24a para. 1 using liquefied natural gas as fuel, related to the application of operational requirements for such a vessel, the identification, assessment, and management of risks associated with such operation, the safe operation of the liquefied natural gas propulsion system, its regular inspection and maintenance, and the bunkering of liquefied natural gas (hereinafter referred to as "liquefied natural gas expert activity") may only be performed by a person who has been granted authorization to do so.

(3) A floating machine performing work on a waterway, whose movement on the waterway is solely related to the performance of this work, may only be operated by a person who has been granted authorization to do so, or by a person who is gaining experience in operating a floating machine under the supervision of a holder of a valid machine operator's certificate of competence; in such a case, the floating machine does not need to be led by a master.

(4) Authorization to perform passenger transport expert activity or liquefied natural gas expert activity may be evidenced by a valid

- a) European Union certificate of expert qualification issued for the performance of this activity by the navigation authority,

- b) European Union certificate of expert qualification issued for the performance of this activity by another Member State of the European Union,

- c) European Union certificate of expert qualification issued for the performance of this activity by a contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area,

- d) certificate of expert qualification issued for the performance of this activity under the Revised Convention for Rhine Navigation by a state that is a contracting party thereto, or

- e) certificate of expert qualification issued for the performance of this activity by another state, if this certificate is recognized under a Commission regulation.

(5) Authorization to perform passenger transport expert activity on a merchant sea-going vessel registered in a maritime register or a similar register maintained by another state, which is operated on a waterway of the Czech Republic, may also be evidenced by a valid certificate of competence

- a) issued or recognized by the Ministry of Transport, another Member State of the European Union, or a contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area and

- b) demonstrating authorization to perform the function of a crew member of such a vessel intended for the carriage of more than 12 passengers.

(6) Authorization to perform liquefied natural gas expert activity on a merchant sea-going vessel registered in a maritime register or a similar register maintained by another state, which is operated on a waterway of the Czech Republic, may also be evidenced by a valid certificate of competence

- a) issued or recognized by the Ministry of Transport, another Member State of the European Union, or a contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area and

- b) demonstrating authorization to perform the function of a crew member of such a vessel using liquefied natural gas as fuel.

(7) Authorization to operate a floating machine performing work on a waterway, whose movement on the waterway is solely related to the performance of this work (hereinafter referred to as "floating machine operation"), may be evidenced by a valid machine operator's certificate of competence issued by the navigation authority.

(8) The operator of a vessel intended for the carriage of more than 12 passengers is obliged to ensure that passenger transport expert activity is performed by a person who holds a valid document proving authorization to perform passenger transport expert activity. The operator of a vessel specified in § 24a para. 1 using liquefied natural gas as fuel is obliged to ensure that liquefied natural gas expert activity is performed by a person who holds a valid document proving authorization to perform liquefied natural gas expert activity. The operator of a floating machine is obliged to ensure that floating machine operation is performed by a person who holds a valid machine operator's certificate, or by a person who is gaining experience in operating a floating machine under the supervision of a holder of a valid machine operator's certificate of competence.

p24o.heading: Passenger transport expert
p24o.text:
(1) The Navigation Authority shall, upon request, grant authorization to perform the activity of a "passenger transport expert" to a natural person who has reached the age of 18 and has successfully passed an examination of practical skills required for the performance of the activity of a passenger transport expert. The Navigation Authority shall not grant authorization to perform the activity of a passenger transport expert if another Member State of the European Union or another contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area has already issued a European Union certificate of qualification for a passenger transport expert.

(2) After the submission of an application for authorization to perform the activity of a passenger transport expert, the Navigation Authority shall set a date for the examination of practical skills required for the performance of the activity of a passenger transport expert and suspend the proceedings. If the examination of practical skills is not successfully passed within 1 year from the date of entry into force of the decision by which the proceedings were suspended, the Navigation Authority shall terminate the proceedings.

(3) For the conduct of the examination of practical skills required for the performance of the activity of a passenger transport expert, its taking, the composition of the examination board, the verification of the applicant's identity, and the retaking of the examination, § 24e (1), (2) and (5) shall apply mutatis mutandis. The examination of practical skills shall be conducted by verifying the applicant's practical skills in premises that, by their arrangement and equipment, enable such verification of practical skills.

(4) If the Navigation Authority grants the application for authorization to perform the activity of a passenger transport expert, it shall issue, instead of a written decision, a European Union certificate of qualification for a passenger transport expert according to the model established by a directly applicable European Union regulation governing models in the field of professional qualifications in inland navigation^28).

(5) The skills that are the subject of the examination of practical skills required for the performance of the activity of a passenger transport expert, the course of this examination, the method of its evaluation, the conditions for appointment as an examiner, and the arrangement and equipment of premises that enable the verification of practical skills, shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p24p.heading: Validity of the European Union certificate of qualification for a "passenger transport expert" and procedure in case of expiration of its validity period
p24p.text:
(1) The European Union certificate of qualification for a passenger transport expert is valid for a period of 5 years.

(2) The Navigation Authority shall, upon request submitted due to the expiration of the validity period of the European Union certificate of qualification for a passenger transport expert pursuant to § 24n (4) (a) to (c), issue a new certificate instead of a written decision to a natural person who

- a) has successfully passed a new examination of practical skills required for the performance of the activity of a passenger transport expert, or

- b) has completed training intended for the performance of this activity, lasting at least 8 hours.

(3) If the applicant proves the fulfillment of the condition for issuing a new certificate by training and the applicant has completed this training in another Member State of the European Union or a contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area, a document proving the successful completion of training intended for the performance of the activity of a passenger transport expert must be attached to the application. The document proving the successful completion of training may be attached to the application as a copy; in such a case, the Navigation Authority may request the submission of the original document for inspection. If the applicant proves the fulfillment of the condition for issuing a new certificate by training, they shall submit an identity document to the Navigation Authority during the proceedings.

(4) After the submission of the application, the Navigation Authority shall set a date for the examination of practical skills required for the performance of the activity of a passenger transport expert and suspend the proceedings; this shall not apply if the applicant proves the fulfillment of the condition for issuing a new certificate by training. If the examination of practical skills is not successfully passed within 1 year from the date of entry into force of the decision by which the proceedings were suspended, the Navigation Authority shall terminate the proceedings.

(5) The holder is obliged to surrender the original European Union certificate of qualification for a passenger transport expert to the Navigation Authority within 15 days from the date of issue of the new certificate.

p24q.heading: Liquefied natural gas expert
p24q.text:
(1) The navigation authority shall, upon request, grant authorization to perform the activity of a "liquefied natural gas expert" to a natural person who has reached the age of 18 and has successfully passed an examination of practical skills necessary to perform the activity of a "liquefied natural gas expert". The navigation authority shall not grant authorization to perform the activity of a "liquefied natural gas expert" if another Member State of the European Union or another contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area has already issued a European Union certificate of qualification for a "liquefied natural gas expert".

(2) After submitting an application for authorization to perform the activity of a "liquefied natural gas expert", the navigation authority shall set a date for the examination of practical skills necessary to perform the activity of a "liquefied natural gas expert" and suspend the proceedings. If the examination of practical skills is not successfully passed within 1 year from the date on which the decision suspending the proceedings became final, the navigation authority shall terminate the proceedings.

(3) For the conduct of the examination of practical skills necessary to perform the activity of a "liquefied natural gas expert", its taking, the composition of the examination board, the verification of the applicant's identity, and the retaking of the examination, § 24e(1), (2), and (5) shall apply mutatis mutandis. The examination of practical skills shall be conducted by verifying the applicant's practical skills in premises that, by their arrangement and equipment, enable such verification.

(4) If the navigation authority grants the application for authorization to perform the activity of a "liquefied natural gas expert", it shall issue, instead of a written decision, a European Union certificate of qualification for a "liquefied natural gas expert" according to the model established by a directly applicable European Union regulation governing models in the field of professional qualifications in inland navigation^28).

(5) The skills that are the subject of the examination of practical skills necessary to perform the activity of a "liquefied natural gas expert", the course of this examination, the method of its evaluation, the conditions for appointment as an examiner, and the arrangement and equipment of premises that enable the verification of practical skills shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p24r.heading: Validity of the European Union certificate of qualification for a "liquefied natural gas expert" and issuance of a new certificate
p24r.text:
(1) The European Union certificate of qualification for a "liquefied natural gas expert" is valid for a period of 5 years.

(2) The navigation authority shall, upon an application submitted due to the expiry of the validity period of the European Union certificate of qualification for a "liquefied natural gas expert" pursuant to § 24n(4)(a) to (c), issue, instead of a written decision, a new certificate to a natural person who

- a) has successfully passed a new examination of practical skills necessary to perform the activity of a "liquefied natural gas expert", or

- b) has experience in operating a vessel referred to in § 24a(1) that uses liquefied natural gas as fuel, to the extent of at least

- 1. 180 days within 5 years preceding the date of application, or

- 2. 90 days within 1 year preceding the date of application.

(3) If the applicant proves the fulfillment of the condition for issuing a new certificate by experience, the application must be accompanied by a service record book related to the certificate pursuant to § 24a(6) containing a record of this experience confirmed by the navigation authority or another state referred to in § 24a(3)(b) to (e). In such a case, the applicant shall present an identity document to the navigation authority during the proceedings. The service record book may be attached to the application as a copy; in such a case, the navigation authority may request the submission of the original service record book for inspection.

(4) After submitting the application, the navigation authority shall set a date for the examination of practical skills necessary to perform the activity of a "liquefied natural gas expert" and suspend the proceedings; this does not apply if a service record book proving the fulfillment of the condition for issuing a new certificate has been attached to the application. If the examination of practical skills is not successfully passed within 1 year from the date on which the decision suspending the proceedings became final, the navigation authority shall terminate the proceedings.

(5) The holder is obliged to surrender the original European Union certificate of qualification for a "liquefied natural gas expert" to the navigation authority within 15 days from the date of issuance of the new certificate.

p24s.heading: Loss of skills necessary to perform the activity of a "passenger transport expert" or the activity of a "liquefied natural gas expert"
p24s.text:
(1) If the navigation authority has reasonable suspicion that the holder of a European Union certificate of qualification for a passenger transport expert or a European Union certificate of qualification for a liquefied natural gas expert issued by the navigation authority has lost the skills necessary to perform the activity of a passenger transport expert or a liquefied natural gas expert, it shall invite them in writing, stating the reasons for its suspicion, to undergo a practical skills re-examination by a specified deadline, and shall instruct them in the invitation about the consequences of not undergoing the re-examination. The holder of this certificate is obliged to undergo the practical skills re-examination by the specified deadline.

(2) For the conduct of the practical skills re-examination, the place of re-examination, the composition of the examination board, the proof of identity of the person undergoing the re-examination, and the repetition of the re-examination, § 24e (1), (2) and (5) shall apply mutatis mutandis. The practical skills re-examination shall be carried out by verifying the practical skills of the person undergoing the re-examination in premises that, by their arrangement and equipment, enable such verification.

(3) In the event of reasonable suspicion that the loss of skills necessary to perform the activity of a passenger transport expert or a liquefied natural gas expert by the holder of a certificate under § 24n (4) may seriously endanger the safety of navigation, the navigation authority shall prohibit them from performing the activity of a passenger transport expert or a liquefied natural gas expert. An appeal against this decision shall not have suspensive effect. If the reasons for which this decision was issued cease to exist, the navigation authority shall revoke the decision.

(4) If the holder of a European Union certificate of qualification for a passenger transport expert or a European Union certificate of qualification for a liquefied natural gas expert does not undergo the re-examination by the specified deadline or fails this re-examination, the navigation authority shall withdraw their authorization to perform the activity of a passenger transport expert or a liquefied natural gas expert.

(5) If the navigation authority has decided to withdraw the authorization to perform the activity of a passenger transport expert or a liquefied natural gas expert, the holder of the European Union certificate of qualification for a passenger transport expert or the European Union certificate of qualification for a liquefied natural gas expert is obliged to surrender this document to the navigation authority within 15 days from the date the decision becomes final.

(6) The skills that are the subject of the practical skills re-examination necessary to perform the activity of a passenger transport expert or a liquefied natural gas expert, the course of the re-examination, the method of its evaluation, the conditions for appointment as an examination commissioner, and the arrangement and equipment of premises that enable the verification of practical skills, shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p24t.heading: Loss, destruction, theft or damage of a European Union certificate of qualification for a passenger transport expert or a European Union certificate of qualification for a liquefied natural gas expert, or a change in the data contained therein
p24t.text:
The holder of a European Union certificate of qualification for a passenger transport expert or a European Union certificate of qualification for a liquefied natural gas expert is obliged to report the loss, destruction, theft or damage of the certificate, or a change in their first name, or names, or surname, to the navigation authority within 1 month from the date on which this event occurred; in the case of theft of the certificate or a change in the first name, or names, or surname, they shall simultaneously submit documents proving this fact. If the notifier requests it, the navigation authority shall issue a new certificate with a validity period corresponding to the validity period of the original document. If the original certificate has been damaged or the first name, or names, or surname of its holder has changed, the notifier is obliged to surrender the original document to the navigation authority within 15 days from the date of

- a) issuance of a new certificate, or

- b) reporting of this fact, if a new certificate is not requested.

p24u.heading: Engine Master
p24u.text:
(1) The navigation authority shall, upon request, grant authorization to operate a floating machine to a natural person who is medically fit, has reached the age of 18, has experience in operating a floating machine gained under the supervision of a holder of a valid engine master's certificate of competence for at least 60 days, and has successfully passed an examination of theoretical knowledge necessary for operating a floating machine.

(2) The application for authorization to operate a floating machine must be accompanied by a medical report proving the applicant's medical fitness and a confirmation of the required experience issued by the operator of the floating machine. These documents may be attached to the application as copies; in such a case, the navigation authority may request the submission of the original document for inspection.

(3) After submitting the application for authorization to operate a floating machine, the navigation authority shall set a date for the examination of theoretical knowledge necessary for operating a floating machine and suspend the proceedings. If the examination of theoretical knowledge is not successfully passed within 1 year from the date the decision to suspend the proceedings becomes final, the navigation authority shall terminate the proceedings.

(4) For the conduct of the examination of theoretical knowledge necessary for operating a floating machine, its taking, the composition of the examination board, the proof of identity of the applicant, and the repetition of the examination, § 24e (1) and (2), § 24e (3) (a), and § 24e (5) shall apply mutatis mutandis.

(5) If the navigation authority grants the application for authorization to operate a floating machine, it shall issue an engine master's certificate of competence instead of a written decision.

(6) The knowledge that is the subject of the examination of theoretical knowledge necessary for operating a floating machine, the course of this examination, the method of its evaluation, the conditions for appointment as an examination commissioner, and the model of the engine master's certificate of competence shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p24v.heading: Validity of the engine master's certificate of competence and procedure in case of expiry of its validity
p24v.text:
(1) A master mechanic's certificate of competency shall be valid

- a) until the day preceding the day on which its holder reaches the age of 60 years,

- b) for a period of 5 years, but no later than the day preceding the day on which its holder reaches the age of 70 years, if it certifies authorization to operate a floating machine for the period after reaching the age of 60 years, or

- c) for a period of 2 years, if it certifies authorization to operate a floating machine for the period after reaching the age of 70 years.

(2) The navigation authority shall, upon request submitted due to the expiry of the validity period of a master mechanic's certificate of competency, mark its new validity period in this certificate, if the applicant is medically fit. The holder is obliged to submit the certificate to the navigation authority for this purpose.

(3) The application pursuant to paragraph 2 must be accompanied by a new medical report certifying the applicant's medical fitness. The new medical report may be attached to the application in copy; in such a case, the navigation authority may require the submission of the original report for inspection. If the navigation authority complies, it shall not issue a written decision.

p24w.heading: Medical fitness of a master mechanic
p24w.text:
(1) The “medical fitness” of a master mechanic means their physical and mental ability to operate a floating machine.

(2) If the medical report indicates that the applicant for authorization to operate a floating machine is medically fit with a condition, the navigation authority shall condition the authorization to operate a floating machine and mark this condition in the master mechanic's certificate of competency. If the medical report, with regard to the ascertained health status of the applicant for authorization to operate a floating machine, was issued for a limited period, the navigation authority shall mark the period of its validity in the master mechanic's certificate of competency so that it corresponds to this limited period; this shall not apply if, pursuant to § 24v para. 1, the validity period of the certificate would be shorter.

(3) If the operator or master of a floating machine or the navigation authority has reasonable suspicion that there has been a change in the health status of the holder of a master mechanic's certificate of competency that could lead to the loss or conditioning of their medical fitness, they shall send a written request, stating the reasons for their suspicion, for the holder to undergo a medical examination. If the request was made by the operator or master of the vessel, they shall also send it to the navigation authority without undue delay. The holder of the master mechanic's certificate of competency is obliged, within 1 month from the date of delivery of the request,

- a) to undergo a medical examination and

- b) to inform the navigation authority which assessing physician performed the medical examination.

(4) In case of reasonable suspicion that a change in the health status of the holder of a master mechanic's certificate of competency may seriously endanger navigation safety, the navigation authority shall prohibit them from operating a floating machine. An appeal against this decision shall not have suspensive effect. If the reasons for which this decision was issued cease to exist, the navigation authority shall revoke the decision.

(5) The assessing physician shall send the medical report issued on the basis of the medical examination without undue delay to the assessed person, to the operator of the floating machine operated by the assessed person, if known to them, and to the navigation authority.

(6) If the medical report issued on the basis of the medical examination, which the holder of a master mechanic's certificate of competency underwent based on a request pursuant to paragraph 3, indicates that they are medically fit with a condition that is not yet marked in the master mechanic's certificate of competency, the navigation authority shall condition the authorization to operate a floating machine and mark this condition in the certificate. If the medical report, with regard to the ascertained health status of the holder of a master mechanic's certificate of competency, was issued for a limited period, the navigation authority shall mark the period of its new validity in the certificate so that it corresponds to this limited period; this shall not apply if, pursuant to § 24v para. 1, the validity period of the certificate would be shorter. In these cases, the holder of the master mechanic's certificate of competency is obliged to submit the certificate to the navigation authority within 10 days from the date of receipt of the medical report.

(7) If the holder of a master mechanic's certificate of competency fails to inform the navigation authority within the stipulated period which assessing physician performed the medical examination based on a request pursuant to paragraph 3, the navigation authority shall request them to provide this information within 15 days from the date of delivery of the repeated request, with instruction on the consequences in case of non-compliance.

(8) If the holder of a master mechanic's certificate of competency fails to inform, even within the period stipulated in the repeated request, which assessing physician performed the medical examination, or if the navigation authority ascertains that they did not undergo a medical examination with the physician stated in the notification, or if the medical report issued on the basis of the medical examination indicates that they are medically unfit to operate a floating machine, the navigation authority shall revoke their authorization to operate a floating machine and, if a decision was made to prohibit the activity consisting of operating a floating machine, it shall revoke this decision. If the navigation authority decided to revoke the authorization to operate a floating machine, the holder of the master mechanic's certificate of competency is obliged to surrender this certificate to the navigation authority within 15 days from the date the decision becomes final.

(9) Diseases, defects and conditions that exclude or condition the medical fitness of a master mechanic or are a reason for issuing a medical report for a limited period, the types of examinations and their content, and the requirements for a medical report shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p24x.heading: Loss of knowledge required to operate a floating machine
p24x.text:
(1) If the navigation authority has reasonable suspicion that the holder of a master engineer's certificate of competence has lost the knowledge necessary to operate a floating machine, it shall send a written request to the holder, stating the reasons for its suspicion, to undergo a re-examination of theoretical knowledge by a specified deadline, and shall instruct the holder in the request about the consequences of failing to undergo the re-examination. The holder of a master engineer's certificate of competence is obliged to undergo a re-examination of theoretical knowledge by the specified deadline.

(2) For the purpose of conducting the re-examination of theoretical knowledge, the place and form of the re-examination, the composition of the examination board, the verification of the identity of the person undergoing the re-examination, and the repetition of the re-examination, § 24e para. 1 and 2, § 24e para. 3 letter a), and § 24e para. 5 shall apply mutatis mutandis.

(3) In the event of reasonable suspicion that the loss of knowledge necessary to operate a floating machine by the holder of a master engineer's certificate of competence may seriously endanger navigation safety, the navigation authority shall prohibit the holder from engaging in activities involving the operation of a floating machine. An appeal against this decision shall not have suspensive effect. If the reasons for which this decision was issued cease to exist, the navigation authority shall revoke the decision.

(4) If the holder of a master engineer's certificate of competence fails to undergo the re-examination by the specified deadline or fails this re-examination, the navigation authority shall withdraw their authorization to operate a floating machine.

(5) If the navigation authority has decided to withdraw the authorization to operate a floating machine, the holder of a master engineer's certificate of competence is obliged to surrender this document to the navigation authority within 15 days from the date the decision becomes final.

(6) The knowledge that is the subject of the re-examination of theoretical knowledge necessary to operate a floating machine, the course of the re-examination, the method of its evaluation, and the conditions for appointing an examination commissioner shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p24y.heading: Loss, destruction, theft or damage of a master engineer's certificate of competence or a change in the data contained therein
p24y.text:
The holder of a master engineer's certificate of competence is obliged to report the loss, destruction, theft or damage of the certificate, or a change of their first name(s) or surname, to the navigation authority within 1 month from the date on which this event occurred; in the case of theft of the certificate or a change of first name(s) or surname, they shall simultaneously submit documents proving this fact. If the reporting person requests it, the navigation authority shall issue a new certificate with a validity period corresponding to the validity period of the original document. If the original certificate has been damaged or the first name(s) or surname of its holder has changed, the reporting person is obliged to surrender the original document to the navigation authority within 15 days from the date of

- a) issuance of the new certificate, or

- b) reporting of this fact, if a new certificate is not requested.

p24z.heading: Obligation to carry documents when operating a vessel
p24z.text:
(1) A crew member of a vessel specified in § 24a para. 1 is obliged to carry a valid document proving the authorization to perform the relevant function of a vessel crew member when operating the vessel; the master of a vessel,

- a) which navigates using radar or ensures the movement of a pushed convoy of vessels with an area of at least 7,000 m^2, must also have an entry in this document proving their special authorization to perform the function of master of such a vessel,

- b) which uses liquefied natural gas as fuel, is also obliged to carry a valid document proving the authorization to perform the activity of a liquefied natural gas expert when operating the vessel, or

- c) who is the holder of a certificate of qualification of a vessel master according to § 24a para. 6, is also obliged to carry a service record book when operating this vessel.

(2) A person performing the activity of an expert in passenger transport is obliged to carry a valid document proving the authorization to perform the activity of an expert in passenger transport when operating a vessel intended for the transport of more than 12 passengers.

(3) A person performing the activity of an expert in liquefied natural gas is obliged to carry a valid document proving the authorization to perform the activity of an expert in liquefied natural gas when operating a vessel specified in § 24a para. 1 using liquefied natural gas as fuel.

(4) A master engineer is obliged to carry a valid master engineer's certificate of competence when operating a floating machine.

p25.heading: Training intended for a vessel crew member or for performing the activity of an expert in passenger transport
p25.text:
(1) Training intended for a vessel crew member or for the performance of activities of a passenger transport expert may only be undertaken with a person authorized to provide it by

- a) the navigation authority,

- b) another Member State of the European Union, or

- c) a contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area.

(2) The navigation authority may, upon request, authorize a person to provide training if that person

- a) has its registered office in the territory of a Member State of the European Union,

- b) has the right to use non-residential premises necessary for providing the training,

- c) is the operator of a vessel whose technical parameters and equipment allow for the provision of training, or has a contractual relationship with such an operator, the subject of which is the use of such a vessel for the purpose of providing training,

- d) has secured teaching materials, aids, and other equipment necessary for providing the training,

- e) has secured a simulator whose technical capability has been approved by the navigation authority, another Member State of the European Union, or a contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area, if it intends to use the simulator to verify the practical skills of a vessel master,

- f) has developed a plan for providing the training, containing a definition of the organization, scope, and method of providing the training and its control,

- g) has implemented a quality management system in accordance with the harmonized Czech technical standard regulating quality management^31),

- h) is financially capable to the extent necessary for providing the training,

- i) has ensured the provision of training and final examinations through professionally qualified natural persons, and

- j) has ensured the exclusion of conflicts of interest during the final examinations.

(3) A person is financially capable of providing training if they are able to financially secure its provision for a period of at least 3 years. A person is not considered financially capable if

- a) their insolvency is being resolved by bankruptcy or reorganization based on a decision of the insolvency court,

- b) the insolvency court has, within 5 years before the date of filing the application for authorization, validly decided to cancel the bankruptcy due to the debtor's assets being entirely insufficient to satisfy creditors,

- c) they have an overdue payment registered with the authorities of the Financial Administration of the Czech Republic or the Customs Administration of the Czech Republic, with the exception of an overdue payment for which a deferral of payment or payment in installments has been permitted,

- d) they have an overdue payment registered for insurance premiums or penalties for social security or for contributions to state employment policy, with the exception of an overdue payment for which payment in installments has been permitted and they are not in default with installment payments, or

- e) they have an overdue payment registered for insurance premiums or penalties for public health insurance.

(4) Professionally qualified to provide training and final examinations is

- a) a teacher of a secondary, higher vocational, or university school teaching a subject whose instruction requires knowledge and skills usable for providing training and final examinations, or

- b) a holder of a document proving authorization to perform the function of a vessel master as specified in § 24a par. 1, who has experience in operating such a vessel for at least 500 days and has successfully passed an examination of theoretical knowledge and practical skills necessary for providing training.

(5) The application for authorization must be accompanied by

- a) a document proving the fulfillment of the registered office condition, if the applicant has it in another Member State of the European Union,

- b) documents proving the fulfillment of the conditions specified in paragraph 2 letters b) to d),

- c) a document on the approval of the technical capability of the simulator issued by another Member State of the European Union or a contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area, if the applicant intends to use the simulator to verify the practical skills of a vessel master and if the technical capability of the simulator has been approved by that state,

- d) a plan for providing the training by the applicant, containing a definition of the organization, scope, and method of providing the training and its control,

- e) a document proving the implementation of a quality management system in accordance with the harmonized Czech technical standard regulating quality management^31),

- f) documents proving the fulfillment of the applicant's financial capability condition, which are a document proving the volume of available financial resources of the applicant, including the status of bank accounts and loans, and their

- 1. latest financial statements, if the applicant keeps accounts,

- 2. opening balance sheet or opening balance sheet in simplified scope, if the applicant will keep accounts, or

- 3. opening overview of business assets, if the applicant will keep tax records,

- g) documents proving the professional qualification of the persons who are to provide the training and final examinations, and documents proving the contractual relationship of these persons to the applicant, and

- h) a document proving the method of ensuring the exclusion of conflicts of interest during the final examinations.

(6) In the authorization, the navigation authority shall specify the scope of training to be provided by the authorized person and the conditions for ensuring its proper provision.

(7) The authorized person is obliged to

- a) ensure that persons providing training and final examinations are familiar with the plan for providing the training, containing a definition of the organization, scope, and method of providing the training and its control,

- b) provide training through professionally qualified natural persons and in a manner and scope that ensures the acquisition of theoretical knowledge and practical skills by the vessel crew member or passenger transport expert,

- c) provide training in premises and using a vessel, teaching materials, aids, and other equipment that ensures the acquisition of theoretical knowledge and practical skills by the vessel crew member or passenger transport expert,

- d) provide training using a simulator whose technical capability has been approved by the navigation authority, another Member State of the European Union, or a contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area, and for which no final decision on technical unsuitability or prohibition of using the simulator for verifying practical skills under § 25b par. 5 has been issued, if the simulator is used to verify the practical skills of a vessel master,

- e) keep a list of persons participating in the provided training and send this list immediately after the start of the training via the public data network to the data box of the navigation authority,
