- f) subject persons participating in the provided training to a final examination that verifies the acquisition of theoretical knowledge and practical skills by the vessel crew member or passenger transport expert, and conduct this examination through professionally qualified natural persons,

- g) draw up a record of the course and result of the training and final examination, which shall be kept for 5 years from the date of the final examination,

- h) communicate the result of the final examination immediately after its completion via the public data network to the data box of the navigation authority, and

- i) issue a certificate of successful completion of the final examination to the training graduate.

(8) The navigation authority shall withdraw the authorization if the authorized person

- a) ceases to meet any of the conditions for authorization,

- b) seriously violates an obligation stipulated by this Act or a condition stipulated in the authorization, or

- c) requests it.

(9) The theoretical knowledge and practical skills, the acquisition of which must be ensured by training intended for a vessel crew member or for the performance of activities of a passenger transport expert, the manner and course of providing this training, the detailed breakdown of data that must be contained in the plan for its provision, the technical parameters and equipment of the vessel that allow for the provision of training, and the extent of financial capability necessary for its provision shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p25a.heading: Examination of theoretical knowledge and practical skills required for conducting training
p25a.text:
(1) The examination of theoretical knowledge and the examination of practical skills, which prove professional competence for conducting training intended for a vessel crew member or for performing the activity of a passenger transport expert and for conducting the final examination, shall be carried out by the navigation authority.

(2) The examination of theoretical knowledge and the examination of practical skills, which prove professional competence for conducting training and the final examination, shall be taken at a place designated by the navigation authority before an examination board, which shall consist of at least three members and be composed of examiners appointed and dismissed by the navigation authority. A person whose participation in the examination would, in view of their relationship to the applicant, jeopardize the impartial conduct of the examination, may not be a member of the examination board.

(3) The examination of theoretical knowledge, which proves professional competence for conducting training and the final examination, shall be conducted in the form of an oral examination.

(4) The examination of practical skills, which proves professional competence for conducting training and the final examination, shall be conducted on a vessel whose technical parameters and equipment allow for the verification of this competence.

(5) The knowledge and skills that are the subject of the examination, which proves professional competence for conducting training intended for a vessel crew member or for performing the activity of a passenger transport expert and for conducting the final examination, the course of conducting this examination, the method of its evaluation, the conditions for appointment as an examination commissioner, and the technical parameters and equipment of the vessel that allow for the verification of professional competence for conducting training and the final examination, shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p25b.heading: Simulators
p25b.text:
(1) The navigation authority shall, upon request by a person intending to use a simulator to verify the practical skills of a vessel master or the practical skills required for navigating a vessel using radar, approve its technical suitability, provided the simulator meets the conditions for technical suitability.

(2) In addition to the general requirements under the Administrative Procedure Code, the request shall include information on whether the applicant intends to use the simulator to verify the practical skills of a vessel master or the practical skills required for navigating a vessel using radar.

(3) The navigation authority shall verify compliance with the technical suitability conditions of the simulator by means of a technical inspection. To conduct the technical inspection, the navigation authority shall establish an expert commission. A person whose participation in the inspection would, in view of their relationship to the applicant, jeopardize the impartial conduct of the inspection, may not be a member of the expert commission. The expert commission conducting the technical inspection shall draw up a report on its outcome.

(4) In the decision on the approval of the technical suitability of the simulator, the navigation authority shall state whether the technical suitability of the simulator has been approved for verifying the practical skills of a vessel master or the practical skills required for navigating a vessel using radar.

(5) If a simulator whose technical suitability has been approved by the navigation authority does not meet the conditions for technical suitability, the navigation authority shall decide on its technical unsuitability; an appeal against this decision shall not have suspensive effect. If a simulator whose technical suitability has been approved by another Member State of the European Union or a contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area does not meet the conditions for technical suitability, the navigation authority shall prohibit the use of the simulator for verifying the practical skills of a vessel master or the practical skills required for navigating a vessel using radar; an appeal against this decision shall not have suspensive effect.

(6) The navigation authority shall notify the Commission and the relevant international organizations active in inland navigation of data concerning decisions on the approval of technical suitability, technical unsuitability, and the prohibition of using a simulator to verify the practical skills of a vessel master or the practical skills required for navigating a vessel using radar, to the extent stipulated by European Union regulation^32).

(7) The conditions for the technical suitability of the simulator, the composition of the expert commission and its modus operandi, the procedure for conducting the technical inspection of the simulator and its scope, and the requirements for the report on the outcome of the technical inspection of the simulator shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p25c.heading: Register of vessel crew members and on-board experts
p25c.text:
(1) The register of crew members of vessels and experts on board vessels is a public administration information system, the administrator of which is the navigation authority.

(2) The register of crew members of vessels and experts on board vessels contains:

- a) data that the navigation authority records in accordance with directly applicable European Union regulations governing models in the field of professional qualifications in inland navigation^28) into the EU certificates of qualification for crew members of vessels, seafarer's service books issued to holders of such certificates, EU certificates of qualification for passenger transport experts, EU certificates of qualification for liquefied natural gas experts, and logbooks issued for vessels specified in § 24a para. 1,

- b) data on certificates according to letter a) whose validity has expired,

- c) data on decisions prohibiting activity consisting of performing the function of a crew member of a vessel or operating a vessel navigating by radar or a vessel that ensures the movement of a pushed convoy of vessels with an area of at least 7,000 m^2, or prohibiting the activity of a passenger transport expert or the activity of a liquefied natural gas expert for holders of certificates according to letter a),

- d) data on decisions that revoked the authorization to perform the function of a crew member of a vessel, special authorization to perform the function of a master of a vessel navigating by radar or a vessel that ensures the movement of a pushed convoy of vessels with an area of at least 7,000 m^2, or authorization to perform the activity of a passenger transport expert or the activity of a liquefied natural gas expert for holders of certificates according to letter a), and

- e) data on certificates according to letter a) that have been lost, destroyed, or stolen.

(3) The navigation authority shall ensure the provision of data maintained in the register of crew members of vessels and experts on board vessels to the Police of the Czech Republic in a manner allowing remote and continuous access.

(4) The navigation authority shall ensure the transmission of data maintained in the register of crew members of vessels and experts on board vessels to the extent and in the manner specified by directly applicable European Union regulations governing the European Union database for certificates of qualification, seafarer's service books, and logbooks^33) to:

- a) the European Union database for certificates of qualification and seafarer's service books, and

- b) the European vessel identification number database.

(5) Data maintained in the register of crew members of vessels and experts on board vessels shall be stored until the day on which the navigation authority learns of the death of the holder of an EU certificate of qualification for a crew member of a vessel, an EU certificate of qualification for a passenger transport expert, or an EU certificate of qualification for a liquefied natural gas expert.

p26.heading: Operation of a small or recreational vessel
p26.text:
Only a person who has been granted authorization to do so may operate a small vessel subject to registration under this Act, which is not a vessel according to § 24a para. 1, or a recreational vessel that is not a small vessel; this does not apply to the operation of a vessel:

- a) during an event permitted by the navigation authority on a monitored waterway in a designated part of that waterway,

- b) during the performance of a practical skills test necessary for operating such a vessel, or

- c) by a person who has reached the age of 18, if it is a small vessel with its own engine capable of sailing only at a speed not exceeding 15 km/h, where the buoyancy of the vessel is mainly formed by hydrostatic forces and the influence of hydrodynamic forces caused by the speed of the vessel's movement is negligible,

- 1. sailing on a waterway of significant transport use listed in paragraph 1 letter c) of Annex No. 2 to this Act, a special-purpose waterway, or an unmonitored waterway, or

- 2. listed in the list contained in the permit for operating a small vessel rental service.

p26a.heading: Authorization to operate a small vessel
p26a.text:
(1) Upon request, the navigation authority shall grant an authorization to operate a small vessel, which is neither a vessel pursuant to § 24a (1) nor a sailing vessel, on a waterway of the Czech Republic to a natural person who is medically fit, has reached the age of 16, and has successfully passed an examination of theoretical knowledge and an examination of practical skills necessary for operating such a vessel; successful completion of the examination of practical skills is not required if:

- a) the applicant holds a valid certificate of competence for operating a sea yacht issued pursuant to the Maritime Navigation Act, or

- b) the subject of the application is the granting of an authorization to operate a small vessel without its own engine or with its own engine with a power output of up to 20 kW.

(2) Upon request, the navigation authority shall grant an authorization to operate a small vessel pursuant to paragraph 1, extended to include an authorization to operate a sailing vessel on a waterway of the Czech Republic, to a natural person who holds a valid certificate of competence for a small vessel operator proving the authorization pursuant to paragraph 1 or meets the conditions for granting such authorization, and has successfully passed an examination of theoretical knowledge and an examination of practical skills necessary for operating a sailing vessel; successful completion of the examination of practical skills is not required if:

- a) the applicant holds a valid certificate of competence for operating a sea yacht issued pursuant to the Maritime Navigation Act, or

- b) the subject of the application is the granting of an authorization pursuant to paragraph 1 extended to include an authorization to operate a sailing vessel, where the total sail area does not exceed 20 m^2.

(3) If the navigation authority grants an authorization pursuant to paragraph 1 or 2, it shall issue a certificate of competence for a small vessel operator instead of a written decision, indicating the scope of the granted authorization. If the navigation authority has issued a certificate of competence for a small vessel operator to an applicant who already holds such a certificate with a different scope of granted authorization, the applicant is obliged to surrender the original certificate to the navigation authority within 15 days from the date of issuance of the new certificate.

(4) The authorization pursuant to paragraph 1 may also be evidenced by a valid:

- a) certificate pursuant to § 24a (6) proving the authorization to perform the function of a vessel operator,

- b) certificate of competence for a vessel operator proving the authorization to perform the function of a vessel operator with its own engine without power limitation, or

- c) international certificate of competence for a recreational craft operator, if the authorization indicated therein corresponds to the authorization thus evidenced.

(5) The authorization pursuant to paragraph 2 may also be evidenced by a valid international certificate of competence for a recreational craft operator, if the authorization indicated therein corresponds to the authorization thus evidenced.

(6) The navigation authority shall issue an international certificate of competence for a recreational craft operator upon request to a natural person who is a citizen of the Czech Republic, has a residence in the territory of the Czech Republic, or is a citizen of a foreign state not bound by the resolution of the United Nations Economic Commission for Europe regulating the international certificate of competence for a recreational craft operator (hereinafter referred to as the „resolution“), holds a valid certificate of competence for a small vessel operator or meets the conditions for granting an authorization to operate a small vessel, and intends to operate a small vessel, which is a recreational craft, in addition to a waterway of the Czech Republic, also on:

- a) a foreign inland waterway where this certificate is recognized pursuant to the resolution, or

- b) a foreign inland waterway and in coastal sea waters where this certificate is recognized pursuant to the resolution up to 1 nautical mile from the mainland or a coastal island and up to 4° on the Beaufort scale (hereinafter referred to as „coastal sea waters“), provided that they have successfully passed an examination of theoretical knowledge necessary for operating a vessel in coastal sea waters.

(7) The navigation authority shall issue an international certificate of competence for a recreational craft operator instead of a written decision and shall indicate therein the scope of the granted authorization. If the navigation authority has issued an international certificate of competence for a recreational craft operator to an applicant who already holds a certificate of competence for a small vessel operator or an international certificate of competence for a recreational craft operator with a different scope of granted authorization, the applicant is obliged to surrender the original certificate to the navigation authority within 15 days from the date of issuance of the new certificate.

(8) The specimen of the certificate of competence for a small vessel operator and the international certificate of competence for a recreational craft operator shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p26b.heading: Authorization to operate a recreational craft other than a small vessel
p26b.text:
(1) The Navigation Authority shall, upon request, grant authorization to operate a recreational craft that is not a small craft,

- a) on a waterway of the Czech Republic and on a foreign inland waterway where such authorization is recognized under a resolution, to a natural person who is a citizen of the Czech Republic, has a residence in the territory of the Czech Republic, or is a citizen of a foreign state not bound by the resolution, holds a valid certificate of competence for a small craft master or meets the conditions for granting authorization to operate a small craft, has reached the age of 18, and has successfully passed an examination of practical skills necessary for operating such a craft,

- b) on a waterway of the Czech Republic, on a foreign inland waterway, and in coastal sea waters, to a natural person who is a citizen of the Czech Republic, has a residence in the territory of the Czech Republic, or is a citizen of a foreign state not bound by the resolution,

- 1. holds a valid certificate of competence for a small craft master or meets the conditions for granting authorization to operate a small craft, has reached the age of 18, and has successfully passed an examination of practical skills necessary for operating such a craft and an examination of theoretical knowledge necessary for operating a craft in coastal sea waters, or

- 2. holds a valid international certificate of competence for a recreational craft master issued by the Navigation Authority proving authorization under letter a) and has successfully passed an examination of theoretical knowledge necessary for operating a craft in coastal sea waters.

(2) If the Navigation Authority grants authorization under paragraph 1, it shall issue an international certificate of competence for a recreational craft master instead of a written decision and shall indicate the scope of the granted authorization therein. If the Navigation Authority has issued an international certificate of competence for a recreational craft master to an applicant who already holds a certificate of competence for a small craft master or an international certificate of competence for a recreational craft master with a different scope of granted authorization, the applicant is obliged to surrender the original certificate to the Navigation Authority within 15 days from the date of issue of the new certificate.

(3) Authorization to operate a recreational craft that is neither a small craft nor a sailing vessel on a waterway of the Czech Republic may also be evidenced by a valid

- a) certificate under § 24a (6) proving authorization to perform the function of a craft master, or

- b) certificate of competence for a craft master proving authorization to perform the function of a craft master with its own engine without power limitation.

p26c.heading: Application for granting authorization to operate a small craft, application for issuing an international certificate of competence for a recreational craft master, application for granting authorization to operate a recreational craft other than a small craft, and procedure after submitting the application
p26c.text:
(1) An application under § 26a (1), (2) or (6) or § 26b (1) must be accompanied by

- a) a medical report proving the applicant's health fitness; this does not apply if the applicant holds a valid certificate of competence for a small craft master or a valid international certificate of competence for a recreational craft master, and

- b) a valid certificate of competence for operating a sea yacht issued under the Maritime Navigation Act, if the applicant holds it and intends to use its submission to replace the successful passing of an examination of practical skills necessary for operating a small craft.

(2) The documents listed in paragraph 1 may be attached to the application as copies; in such a case, the Navigation Authority may request the submission of the original document for inspection.

(3) If a condition for granting an application under § 26a (1), (2) or (6) or § 26b (1) is the successful passing of an examination of practical skills necessary for operating a small or recreational craft, and this examination was not passed before the date of submission of the application, the Navigation Authority shall discontinue the proceedings. The successful passing of the examination shall not be taken into account if it occurred more than 2 years before the date of submission of the application.

(4) If a condition for granting an application under § 26a (1), (2) or (6) or § 26b (1) is the successful passing of an examination of theoretical knowledge necessary for operating a small craft or a craft in coastal sea waters, the Navigation Authority shall, after the submission of the application, set a date for its performance and suspend the proceedings. If the examination is not successfully passed within 1 year from the date of legal force of the resolution by which the proceedings were suspended, the Navigation Authority shall discontinue the proceedings.

p26d.heading: Examination of theoretical knowledge necessary for operating a small craft or a craft in coastal sea waters
p26d.text:
(1) The examination of theoretical knowledge necessary for operating a small craft or a craft in coastal sea waters shall be conducted by the Navigation Authority. The Navigation Authority shall set the date of the examination so that it can be conducted no later than 3 months from the date of submission of the application. The examination shall be taken at a place designated by the Navigation Authority before an examination board, which shall consist of at least three members appointed and recalled by the director of the Navigation Authority. A person whose participation in the examination would jeopardize the impartial conduct of the examination, given their relationship to the applicant, may not be a member of the examination board.

(2) The examination of theoretical knowledge necessary for operating a small craft or a craft in coastal sea waters shall be taken using computer technology in the form of a test containing questions compiled by random selection from a set of examination questions that the Navigation Authority shall publish on its website.

(3) An applicant shall take the examination of theoretical knowledge necessary for operating a small craft or a craft in coastal sea waters after the Navigation Authority verifies their identity. If the applicant fails the examination, they may repeat it only once in the same proceedings.

(4) The knowledge that is the subject of the examination of theoretical knowledge necessary for operating a small craft or a craft in coastal sea waters, the course of conducting this examination, the method of its evaluation, and the conditions for appointment as an examination commissioner shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p26e.heading: Examination of practical skills necessary for operating a small or recreational craft
p26e.text:
(1) The examination of practical skills required for operating a small or recreational vessel shall be conducted by a person authorized by the navigation authority. The examination shall take place at a location designated by this person and shall verify the practical skills of the applicant in operating a vessel whose technical parameters and equipment allow for such verification.

(2) The authorized person shall

- a) allow the applicant to take the examination after verifying their identity,

- b) draw up a record of the course and result of the examination, which they shall keep for a period of 5 years from the date of its performance,

- c) communicate the result of the examination immediately after its performance to the navigation authority's data box via the public data network, and

- d) issue a certificate to the applicant upon successful completion of the examination.

(3) If a person requests it within 15 days from the day on which they failed the examination of practical skills required for operating a small or recreational vessel with an authorized person, the navigation authority shall conduct this examination itself.

(4) The skills that are the subject of the examination of practical skills required for operating a small or recreational vessel, the course of conducting this examination, the method of its evaluation, the technical parameters and equipment of the vessel that allow for the verification of practical skills required for operating a small or recreational vessel, the content of the record of the course and result of the examination, and the template of the certificate of its successful completion shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p26f.heading: Authorization to conduct the examination of practical skills required for operating a small or recreational vessel
p26f.text:
(1) Upon request, the navigation authority may authorize the conduct of the examination of practical skills required for operating a small or recreational vessel to

- a) a natural person who has their registered office in the territory of a Member State of the European Union, has reached the age of 21, and is professionally qualified to conduct this examination, or

- b) a legal person who has its registered office in the territory of a Member State of the European Union and demonstrates that it will ensure the conduct of this examination through a professionally qualified natural person.

(2) A person is professionally qualified to conduct the examination of practical skills required for operating a small or recreational vessel if they have held a valid small vessel master's certificate or a valid international recreational vessel master's certificate for at least 3 years and have passed the examination of practical skills required to conduct this examination.

(3) The examination proving professional qualification to conduct the examination of practical skills required for operating a small or recreational vessel shall be conducted on a vessel whose technical parameters and equipment allow for the verification of this qualification, and shall take place at a location designated by the navigation authority before an examination board, which shall consist of at least three members appointed and dismissed by the director of the navigation authority. A person whose participation in the examination would jeopardize the impartial course of the examination, given their relationship to the applicant, may not be a member of the examination board.

(4) The navigation authority shall withdraw the authorization if the authorized person

- a) ceases to meet any of the conditions for authorization,

- b) repeatedly or seriously violates an obligation stipulated by this Act, or

- c) requests the withdrawal of the authorization.

(5) The skills that are the subject of the examination proving professional qualification to conduct the examination of practical skills required for operating a small or recreational vessel, the course of conducting this examination, the method of its evaluation, the conditions for appointment as an examination commissioner, and the technical parameters and equipment of the vessel that allow for the verification of this qualification shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p26g.heading: Validity of the small vessel master's certificate and the international recreational vessel master's certificate and procedure after their expiry
p26g.text:
(1) The small vessel master's certificate and the international recreational vessel master's certificate shall be valid

- a) until the day preceding the day on which its holder reaches the age of 65,

- b) until the day preceding the day on which its holder reaches the age of 70, if it evidences the authorization to operate a small or recreational vessel for the period after reaching the age of 65, or

- c) for a period of 2 years, if it evidences the authorization to operate a small or recreational vessel for the period after reaching the age of 70.

(2) Upon a request submitted due to the expiry of the small vessel master's certificate or the international recreational vessel master's certificate, the navigation authority shall issue a new certificate instead of a written decision, provided the applicant is medically fit. The holder is obliged to surrender the original certificate to the navigation authority within 15 days from the date of issuance of the new certificate.

(3) A new medical report evidencing the applicant's medical fitness must be attached to the request pursuant to paragraph 2. The new medical report may be attached to the request as a copy; in such a case, the navigation authority may request the submission of the original report for inspection.

p26h.heading: Medical fitness of a small or recreational vessel master
p26h.text:
(1) The "medical fitness" of a small or recreational craft master means the physical and mental ability to operate a small or recreational craft.

(2) If the medical fitness of the applicant is a condition for granting an application pursuant to § 26a para. 1, 2 or 6 or § 26b para. 1, and the medical report indicates that the applicant is medically fit with a condition, the navigation authority shall make the authorization to operate a small or recreational craft conditional and shall record this condition in the small craft master's certificate of competence or the international recreational craft master's certificate of competence. If the medical report, with regard to the applicant's ascertained health status, was issued for a limited period, the navigation authority shall record the period of its validity in the small craft master's certificate of competence or in the international recreational craft master's certificate of competence so that it corresponds to such limited period; this shall not apply if, pursuant to § 26g para. 1, the period of validity of the certificate would be shorter.

(3) If the navigation authority has reasonable suspicion that a change in the health status of the holder of a small craft master's certificate of competence or an international recreational craft master's certificate of competence has occurred, which could lead to the loss or conditioning of their medical fitness, it shall invite them in writing, stating the reasons for its suspicion, to undergo a medical examination. The holder of a small craft master's certificate of competence or an international recreational craft master's certificate of competence is obliged, within 1 month from the date of delivery of the invitation, to

- a) undergo a medical examination and

- b) inform the navigation authority of the assessing physician with whom they underwent the medical examination.

(4) In the event of reasonable suspicion that the safety of navigation may be seriously endangered by a change in the health status of the holder of a small craft master's certificate of competence or an international recreational craft master's certificate of competence, the navigation authority shall prohibit them from engaging in activities involving the operation of a small or recreational craft. An appeal against this decision shall not have suspensive effect. If the reasons for which this decision was issued cease to exist, the navigation authority shall revoke the decision.

(5) The assessing physician shall send the medical report issued on the basis of the medical examination without undue delay to the assessed person and the navigation authority.

(6) If the medical report issued on the basis of the medical examination, which the holder of a small craft master's certificate of competence or an international recreational craft master's certificate of competence underwent on the basis of an invitation pursuant to paragraph 3, indicates that they are medically fit with a condition that is not yet recorded in the certificate, the navigation authority shall make the authorization to operate a small or recreational craft conditional and shall record this condition in the certificate. If the medical report, with regard to the ascertained health status of the holder of a small craft master's certificate of competence or an international recreational craft master's certificate of competence, was issued for a limited period, the navigation authority shall record the period of its new validity in the certificate so that it corresponds to such limited period; this shall not apply if, pursuant to § 26g para. 1, the period of validity of the certificate would be shorter. The holder of a small craft master's certificate of competence or an international recreational craft master's certificate of competence is obliged in these cases to submit the certificate to the navigation authority within 10 days from the date of receipt of the medical report.

(7) If the holder of a small craft master's certificate of competence or an international recreational craft master's certificate of competence fails to inform the navigation authority within the specified period of the assessing physician with whom they underwent the medical examination on the basis of an invitation pursuant to paragraph 3, the navigation authority shall invite them to provide this information within 15 days from the date of delivery of the repeated invitation, with instruction on the consequences in case of non-compliance.

(8) If the holder of a small craft master's certificate of competence or an international recreational craft master's certificate of competence fails to inform, even within the period specified in the repeated invitation, of the assessing physician with whom they underwent the medical examination, or if the navigation authority ascertains that they did not undergo the medical examination with the physician stated in the notification, or if the medical report issued on the basis of the medical examination indicates that they are medically unfit to operate a small or recreational craft, the navigation authority shall withdraw their authorization to operate a small or recreational craft and, if a decision was made to prohibit activities involving the operation of a small or recreational craft, this decision shall be revoked. If the navigation authority has decided to withdraw the authorization to operate a small or recreational craft, the holder of a small craft master's certificate of competence or an international recreational craft master's certificate of competence is obliged to surrender this certificate to the navigation authority within 15 days from the date of entry into force of the decision.

(9) Diseases, defects and conditions that exclude or condition the medical fitness of a small or recreational craft master or are a reason for issuing a medical report for a limited period, the types of examinations and their content, and the requirements for a medical report shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p26i.heading: Loss of knowledge required for operating a small or recreational craft
p26i.text:
(1) If the navigation authority has reasonable suspicion that the holder of a small craft master's certificate of competence or an international recreational craft master's certificate of competence has lost the knowledge necessary to operate a small or recreational craft, it shall send a written request, stating the reasons for its suspicion, to undergo a theoretical knowledge re-examination by a specified deadline, and shall instruct the holder in the request about the consequences of failing to undergo the re-examination. The holder of a small craft master's certificate of competence or an international recreational craft master's certificate of competence is obliged to undergo the theoretical knowledge re-examination by the specified deadline.

(2) For the conduct of the theoretical knowledge re-examination, the place and form of the re-examination, the composition of the examination board, the verification of the identity of the person undergoing the re-examination, and the repetition of the re-examination, § 26d (1) to (3) shall apply mutatis mutandis.

(3) In the event of reasonable suspicion that the loss of knowledge necessary to operate a small or recreational craft by the holder of a small craft master's certificate of competence or an international recreational craft master's certificate of competence may seriously endanger navigation safety, the navigation authority shall prohibit them from engaging in activities involving the operation of a small or recreational craft. An appeal against this decision shall not have suspensive effect. If the reasons for which this decision was issued cease to exist, the navigation authority shall revoke the decision.

(4) If the holder of a small craft master's certificate of competence or an international recreational craft master's certificate of competence fails to undergo the re-examination by the specified deadline or fails this re-examination, the navigation authority shall revoke their authorization to operate a small or recreational craft.

(5) If the navigation authority has decided to revoke the authorization to operate a small or recreational craft, the holder of a small craft master's certificate of competence or an international recreational craft master's certificate of competence is obliged to surrender this certificate to the navigation authority within 15 days from the date the decision becomes final.

(6) The knowledge that is the subject of the theoretical knowledge re-examination necessary to operate a small or recreational craft, the course of the re-examination, the method of its evaluation, and the conditions for appointing an examination commissioner shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p26j.heading: Loss, destruction, theft or damage of a small craft master's certificate of competence or an international recreational craft master's certificate of competence, or a change in the data contained therein
p26j.text:
The holder of a small craft master's certificate of competence or an international recreational craft master's certificate of competence is obliged to report the loss, destruction, theft or damage of the certificate, or a change in their first name(s) or surname, to the navigation authority within 1 month from the date on which this event occurred; in the case of theft of the certificate or a change in the first name(s) or surname, they shall simultaneously submit documents proving this fact. If requested by the reporting person, the navigation authority shall issue a new certificate with a validity period corresponding to the validity period of the original document. If the original certificate has been damaged or the first name(s) or surname of its holder has changed, the reporting person is obliged to surrender the original document to the navigation authority within 15 days from the date of

- a) issuance of the new certificate, or

- b) reporting this fact, if a new certificate is not requested.

p26k.heading: Obligation to carry documents when operating a small or recreational craft
p26k.text: The master of a small or recreational craft, for the operation of which an authorization according to § 26 (1) is required, is obliged to carry a valid small craft master's certificate of competence or an international recreational craft master's certificate of competence when operating it.

p27.heading: Submission of applications via user interface
p27.text: An application according to § 24b, § 24c, § 24h (1) or (2), § 24j (2) to (4), § 24o (1), § 24p (2), § 24q (1), § 24r (2), § 24u (1), § 24v (2), § 26a (1), (2) or (6), § 26b (1) or § 26g (2) may also be submitted remotely via a user interface specified by the navigation authority; in such a case, the applicant must be identified using electronic identification means according to legal regulations governing electronic identification^34), and § 24d (3), § 24h (6), § 24j (5) third sentence, § 24p (3) third sentence, and § 24r (3) second sentence shall not apply.

p27a.heading: Recognition of professional qualifications for activities in inland navigation
p27a.text:
The procedure according to the Act regulating the recognition of professional qualifications^35) is not affected when it concerns

- a) the performance of the function of a crew member of a vessel, the performance of the function of a master of a vessel that navigates using radar, uses liquefied natural gas as fuel, or ensures the movement of a pushed convoy of vessels with an area of at least 7,000 m^2, or the performance of the activity of a liquefied natural gas expert, if these functions or activities are performed on

- 1. a military vessel^29) operated on a waterway of the Czech Republic or a vessel operated on this waterway by a basic component of the integrated rescue system, municipal police, the navigation authority, or the waterway administrator, or

- 2. a ferry boat attached to a guide rope or chain operated on a waterway of the Czech Republic,

- b) the operation of a floating machine, and

- c) the operation of a small craft that is neither a vessel listed in § 24a (1) nor a recreational craft.

p28.heading: Participant in navigation traffic
p28.text:
A participant in navigation traffic on a waterway is

- a) the master of a vessel,

- b) a crew member of a vessel and other persons on or off the vessel performing activities related to ensuring safe navigation,

- c) a passenger,

- d) a person operating a lock or other navigation facility,

- e) a person swimming or diving in the waterway, and

- f) a person performing an activity on the waterway that may affect the safety and fluidity of navigation traffic.

p29.heading: Duties of a participant in navigation traffic
p29.text:
(1) A participant in navigation shall exercise due caution and behave in such a way as not to endanger the safety and smooth flow of navigation, human life or health, cause damage to property, or cause a navigation accident or an obstruction on the waterway, pollution of the waterway or other environmental damage.

(2) A participant in navigation shall further comply with navigation marks placed on the water surface, banks and on structures on the waterway, and with light and sound signals.

(3) Anyone who has caused an obstruction to navigation on a waterway shall remove it without delay. If they cannot remove the obstruction without delay, they shall notify the waterway administrator and the navigation authority of this fact and visibly mark the location of the obstruction. If the waterway administrator receives notification of an obstruction, they may remove it at the expense of the person who caused it.

p29a.heading: Protection of navigation marks
p29a.text:
(1) No one may damage navigation marks and other navigation facilities placed on a waterway, use them for mooring vessels, or place objects on or near a waterway that are interchangeable with navigation marks.

(2) Navigation marks may only be placed, removed or relocated with the consent of the navigation authority. The consent of the navigation authority is not required if the waterway administrator handles the navigation marks; the waterway administrator is obliged to immediately notify the navigation authority of the placement, removal or relocation of navigation marks carried out by them.

(3) The navigation authority shall issue consent for the placement, removal or relocation of navigation marks if

- a) the safety and smooth flow of navigation on the waterway will not be endangered thereby, and

- b) the placement, removal or relocation of navigation marks does not contradict the purpose of the authorized use of the given section of the waterway.

(4) The owner, lessee or usufructuary lessee of real estate or its part located near a waterway is obliged to tolerate the placement and maintenance of navigation marks by the waterway administrator on such land or structure.

p29b.heading: Duties of the vessel master, vessel crew member and expert on board a vessel

p29c.heading: Fishing gear
p29c.text:
(1) On a monitored waterway, it is prohibited to:

- a) tow fishing gear using several vessels sailing side by side, or

- b) use fishing gear in a port, landing stage or transhipment point.

(2) The prohibition under paragraph 1 letter a) does not apply to research or veterinary work or work related to fish farming, the execution of which the user of the fishing ground notifies the navigation authority at least 30 days in advance. The notification shall include information on the place and time of the work and whether it will be necessary to adjust navigation traffic for its execution.

(3) In the water area defined according to § 30a, it is prohibited to use fishing gear during its operating hours.

p29d.heading: Bathing and Diving
p29d.text:
(1) It is prohibited to bathe and dive in places where this may endanger the safety or smooth flow of navigation or other participants in navigation traffic, especially:

- a) on a ferry route,

- b) on a part of the waterway defined according to § 30a para 1,

- c) within a distance of 100 m from a bridge on a monitored waterway,

- d) within a distance of 100 m from a landing stage, transhipment point, port entrance and the mouth of a tributary that is a monitored waterway,

- e) in a port,

- f) in a lock and in the water area above and below a lock up to the point where the mooring facilities for vessels waiting to pass through end, or

- g) in navigation canals.

(2) The prohibition on diving does not apply to security work, maintenance, construction modifications or changes to waterworks carried out by the waterway administrator.

(3) The navigation authority may, upon request, issue a permit for diving in a place where diving is prohibited, provided that the safety and smooth flow of navigation, or the safety of the person intending to dive in such a place, will not be endangered. The navigation authority shall specify the period of validity of the permit.

(4) A person bathing is obliged to behave in such a way as not to endanger their own safety and the safety and smooth flow of navigation, in particular, they must not cross the path of an approaching vessel, and is obliged to maintain safe distances from vessels.

(5) Paragraphs 1 and 4 shall apply mutatis mutandis to a person bathing using an inflatable mattress or other floating object.

(6) A person who dives outside the area reserved for this activity by navigation markings is obliged to mark the dive site on the surface with a visible safety marking.

p29e.heading: Sailing on a board with a sail or using a traction kite
p29e.text:
(1) The master of a vessel, when sailing on a board with a sail or using a traction kite, is obliged to ensure the safety of persons participating in such sailing and the safety of other participants in navigation traffic.

(2) Sailing on a board with a sail or using a traction kite at night, in reduced visibility, and on sections of transport-significant waterways specified by an implementing legal regulation is prohibited.

(3) The method of ensuring safety when sailing on a board with a sail or using a traction kite shall be determined by an implementing legal regulation.

p29f.heading: Water skiing and similar activities operated by towing behind a vessel
p29f.text:
(1) The master of a vessel towing a water skier or a person engaged in a similar activity by towing behind a vessel is obliged to ensure the safety of the person participating in such activities and the safety of other participants in navigation traffic.

(2) Operating water skiing and similar activities operated by towing behind a vessel at night or in reduced visibility is prohibited.

(3) The method of ensuring safety during water skiing or similar activities shall be determined by an implementing legal regulation.

p29g.heading: Special transport
p29g.text:
(1) The relocation of a floating device on a waterway, the navigation of a floating body, and the navigation of a vessel or a convoy of vessels, if the length, width, height or draught of the vessel or convoy of vessels does not correspond to the parameters of the waterway or structures located thereon, or the vessel or convoy of vessels does not meet the technical fitness conditions or does not have a qualified crew in the number and professional composition according to this Act, (hereinafter referred to as „special transport“) are permitted only on the basis of a permit from the navigation authority. Special transport due to non-compliance with technical fitness conditions may be permitted only in the case of vessels for which compliance with technical fitness conditions is not evidenced by a Union certificate.

(2) The navigation authority shall issue a permit based on an application, provided that special transport will not endanger the safety of navigation traffic and will not cause a risk of damage to the waterway or structures located thereon. The navigation authority shall specify the period for which the permit is issued. The waterway administrator is a party to the proceedings if it concerns the navigation of a vessel or a convoy of vessels whose length, width, height or draught does not correspond to the parameters of the waterway or structures located thereon.

(3) In the permit, the navigation authority shall designate the person ensuring the safe relocation of the floating device, the person entrusted with the guidance of the floating body, or the master of the vessel or convoy of vessels, and shall stipulate conditions to ensure the safety of navigation traffic during special transport.

p29h.heading: Events on a monitored waterway
p29h.text:
(1) An event on a monitored waterway, with the exception of military training or training of integrated rescue system components, which requires adjustment, restriction or interruption of navigation traffic or its special management, may only be organized on the basis of a permit from the navigation authority. The navigation authority shall permit the organization of the event upon the request of its organizer, provided that the safety of navigation is not endangered and other participants in navigation traffic are not unduly restricted. In the permit for organizing the event, the navigation authority shall stipulate conditions to ensure the safety of navigation traffic.

(2) The execution of military training or training of integrated rescue system components is subject to notification to the navigation authority. The notification according to the first sentence must be made at least 1 month in advance. The notification shall include information on the place and time of the training and whether it will be necessary to adjust navigation traffic for the execution of the training.

p29i.heading: Gravel pits
p29i.text:
(1) The operator of a gravel pit is obliged to

- a) secure the part of the gravel pit where navigation is prohibited against entry by unauthorized persons, and

- b) mark the part of the gravel pit where navigation is operated with navigation signs and maintain these navigation signs.

(2) The method of marking with navigation signs in a gravel pit shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p29j.heading: Permit for the use of special signalling on a vessel
p29j.text:
(1) Special signalling on a vessel may only be used on the basis of a permit from the navigation authority. The navigation authority shall issue a permit for the period strictly necessary upon request by the operator of a vessel that is carrying out sounding, measuring or other work activities on a waterway, or that needs to be protected from wave action, if the use of special signalling is necessary

- a) to ensure the safety of the vessel or persons on the vessel,

- b) to secure the work activities being carried out, or

- c) to ensure the safety of navigation.

(2) The navigation authority shall state the period of validity of the permit under paragraph 1.

(3) The types of special signalling on a vessel and the method of their use shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p29k.heading: Details for ensuring safe navigation
p29k.text:
The implementing legal regulation shall stipulate

- a) the method of safe occupancy of a vessel by passengers, loading of a vessel with cargo and its safe distribution on the vessel,

- b) the maximum permissible dimensions of a vessel and a vessel formation for individual sections of a waterway and the maximum permissible draught of a vessel for individual sections of a waterway,

- c) the types of vessels subject to notification before their entry into a lock, a section of a waterway with controlled navigation, a control point or a section of a waterway where navigation signs stipulate the obligation to put a radio station into operation, the content requirements of the notification and the method of its execution,

- d) the types of navigation signs on a waterway and their meaning,

- e) cases in which it is necessary for navigation safety to provide a watch on a vessel or supervision over a vessel, the method of their provision and the method of keeping a record of the provision of a watch or supervision,

- f) the method of storing waste generated on a vessel and the method of its proper disposal from the vessel,

- g) the maximum permissible navigation speeds,

- h) the types of optical and acoustic signalling with which a vessel must be equipped, and the appropriate method of their use,

- i) the method and procedures for ensuring the safety of persons on a vessel and other participants in navigation during navigation, the method of preventing threats or damage to vessels, the waterway or structures located thereon, port facilities and the environment,

- j) the method of equipping a vessel with radiotelephone equipment and other equipment serving to ensure safe navigation and the method of their use,

- k) rescue means and means for proper waste sorting generated on a vessel, with which the vessel operator is obliged to equip the vessel, and

- l) safe distances of bathing persons from vessels and the method of safety marking of the diving person's immersion point.

p29l.text: The provisions of § 29, 29b, 29d and 29j shall apply to rescue or liquidation work of integrated rescue system components and their training, and to military training of the Army of the Czech Republic, only if the purpose of these activities would not be thereby obviously frustrated.

p30.heading: Operation of a foreign vessel
p30.text:
(1) A foreign vessel may be operated on the waterways of the Czech Republic only on the basis of an operating permit, unless an international treaty by which the Czech Republic is bound stipulates otherwise. A vessel for which a vessel certificate or other similar document has been issued by another Member State of the European Union, another contracting State to the Agreement on the European Economic Area or the Swiss Confederation shall not be considered a foreign vessel.

(2) The navigation authority shall decide on the issuance of an operating permit. A condition for the issuance of an operating permit is proof of liability insurance for the operation of the foreign vessel operator. The navigation authority shall issue an operating permit on the basis of the foreign vessel's ship's papers, if it is clear from them that the technical condition of the vessel complies with the technical eligibility conditions. In the event that the technical condition of the foreign vessel does not comply with these conditions, the navigation authority shall issue an operating permit on the basis of an inspection verifying the technical and operational eligibility of the foreign vessel.

(3) The provisions of paragraphs 1 and 2 shall not apply to a foreign small vessel operated by a foreign person for recreational navigation in the Czech Republic.

p30a.heading: Designated water area
p30a.text:
(1) If it is in accordance with special legal regulations governing the protection of surface waters^5e) and if the safety of navigation on the waterway is not endangered thereby, the navigation authority may, by means of a general measure, designate a part of the waterway for a period not exceeding 5 years for

- a) the navigation of small vessels, during which the obligations of the vessel master under § 29b para. 1 letter l) and para. 3 letters a), c), d), e) and n) shall not apply, especially for the navigation of vessels involving bypassing a series of artificially created obstacles, jumping above the water surface, performing other acrobatic activities, or high-speed navigation, or

- b) the operation of water skiing and similar activities performed in tow behind a vessel.

(2) The navigation authority shall, in the general measure, specify the operating hours of the part of the waterway designated according to paragraph 1 (hereinafter referred to as the „designated water area“), the vessels that may be operated in the designated water area, and the maximum permitted number of vessels that may be operated simultaneously in the designated water area.

(3) Written reasoned objections against the draft general measure by which an area is to be designated according to paragraph 1 may also be submitted by the waterway administrator. The municipality in whose cadastral territory the designated water area is to be located is an affected authority according to the Administrative Procedure Code.

(4) The navigation authority may extend the validity of a general measure if the conditions for its issuance are met. The navigation authority shall revoke a general measure if the conditions for its issuance are not met.

(5) The navigation authority shall publish general measures issued according to paragraphs 1 and 4 after their effective date through the river information services system.

(6) The activities listed in paragraph 1 may only be carried out in those parts of the waterway that have been designated for their operation.

(7) It is prohibited to

- a) enter the designated water area if the maximum permitted number of vessels that may be operated simultaneously therein has been reached,

- b) use the designated water area for activities listed in paragraph 1 outside its operating hours, or

- c) enter the designated water area during its operating hours with a vessel for which it was not designated.

(8) The waterway administrator shall mark the designated water area with navigation signs for the period specified by the general measure. The method of marking the designated water area with navigation signs shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p30b.heading: Small Vessel Rental Service
p30b.text:
(1) A small vessel rental service located on a waterway (hereinafter referred to as the „rental service“) may only be operated on the basis of a permit from the navigation authority issued upon the application of a natural or legal person intending to operate it. In addition to the general requirements for submissions, the application shall contain the designation of the waterway and the proposed location of the rental service on this waterway. The application shall be accompanied by

- a) the operating rules of the rental service,

- b) a flood plan, if there is an obligation to prepare one according to the Water Act,

- c) a list of vessels that the applicant intends to rent, and

- d) a user manual for the vessels that the applicant intends to rent.

(2) The navigation authority shall permit the operation of the rental service if

- a) the proposed location of the rental service on the waterway, its operation, and the operation of the vessels that the applicant intends to rent will not endanger the safety and smooth flow of navigation, and

- b) the vessels that the applicant intends to rent meet the conditions of technical fitness and are suitable for rental and operation on the section of the waterway specified in the rental service's operating rules, with regard to the safety and smooth flow of navigation.

(3) The waterway administrator is a party to the proceedings for the issuance of a permit to operate a small vessel rental service.

(4) In the permit, the navigation authority shall specify the conditions serving to ensure the safe operation of the rental service and a list of vessels that may be rented at the rental service. Renting a vessel other than that listed in the permit is only possible with the consent of the navigation authority. The navigation authority shall issue consent upon the application of the permit holder if this vessel meets the conditions of technical fitness and is suitable for rental and operation on the section of the waterway specified in the rental service's operating rules, with regard to the safety and smooth flow of navigation.

(5) The permit holder shall promptly inform the navigation authority of any change in the facts stated in the application and in the documents attached thereto.

(6) The navigation authority shall revoke the issued permit if the conditions for its issuance are not met or if the permit holder

- a) seriously violates the provisions of this Act,

- b) seriously violates the conditions stipulated in the permit, or

- c) has requested the revocation of the permit.

(7) The substantive requirements for the operating rules of the rental service, the list of vessels that may be rented at the rental service, and the user manual for individual types of vessels shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p31.heading: Accident in waterway traffic
p31.text:
(1) An accident is an event that occurred in navigation on a waterway or in causal connection therewith and that resulted in damage to or destruction of a vessel, damage to or deterioration of transported cargo, if these consequences did not result from the nature of the cargo itself, further damage to the waterway or a structure thereon, injury to health or death of participants in navigation on the waterway.

(2) The master of a vessel and the operator of a vessel are obliged to ensure that an accident that occurred on a monitored waterway or, if a vessel subject to registration under this Act is involved, on an unmonitored waterway, is immediately reported to the navigation authority responsible for its expert investigation and determination of causes. In the event that the accident resulted in water pollution or a threat to its quality or damage to the waterway, its component or a structure thereon, the master of the vessel and the operator of the vessel are also obliged to report the accident to the relevant water management authority and the waterway administrator. The reporting obligation does not apply to an accident in which only one vessel is involved and which resulted in damage only to that vessel or its transported cargo.

(3) A vessel involved in an accident where the consequences of the accident extended beyond the scope of the vessel must not leave the scene of the accident without the consent of the navigation authority. If the underwater part of the vessel was damaged in the accident or if there is reasonable suspicion that such damage occurred, the master of the vessel is obliged to immediately move the vessel to the nearest shallows or another safe place.

Sacrifice of property in common danger

p31a.text:
(1) If a vessel, persons or goods being transported are in common danger, the master of the vessel may decide on extraordinary measures concerning the vessel or the goods being transported in order to avert impending damage. Everyone who benefited from this shall provide proportional compensation to the injured party.

(2) No claim for damages shall be held by a person who intentionally or through gross negligence failed to declare their goods for transport or declared them incorrectly. The first sentence shall not apply to goods carried by the person being transported. However, if the goods according to the first sentence were saved, their owner shall participate in the compensation.

(3) If the actual value of the damaged or destroyed goods was higher than the declared value, the declared value shall be decisive for compensation for damage to such goods. If such goods were saved, their owner shall participate in the compensation according to their actual value.

p31b.text:
(1) The vessel operator shall without undue delay publish in the Commercial Bulletin information on the facts according to § 31a (1) first sentence and the proposal of the person who will prepare the average adjustment (hereinafter referred to as the „average adjuster“); for these purposes, average adjustment means the apportionment of the participation of individual persons in the compensation for incurred damages. The vessel operator shall further take necessary measures for the preparation of the average adjustment.

(2) If any of the interested parties files objections against the proposed average adjuster with the vessel operator within 30 days from the date of its publication in the Commercial Bulletin, the court shall appoint the average adjuster upon its proposal; in doing so, it shall take into account the objections filed. The proposal for the appointment of an average adjuster by the court must be filed within 15 days from the date of expiry of the period according to the first sentence. The court shall preferentially appoint a person who has been named as an average adjuster by the Economic Chamber of the Czech Republic; if this is not possible, it shall appoint another suitable person.

(3) The average adjuster shall prepare the average adjustment in writing and publish it in the Commercial Bulletin. The costs of its preparation shall be taken into account in the average adjustment.

(4) Anyone whom the average adjustment concerns may file an action for its annulment within 30 days from the date of publication of the average adjustment. If they fail to do so, the average adjustment shall acquire the effects of a final court decision upon the expiry of this period.

(5) If the court annuls the average adjustment, the average adjuster is obliged to prepare a new average adjustment; in doing so, they are bound by the legal opinion of the court. If the proceedings for the annulment of the average adjustment conclude otherwise, the average adjustment becomes enforceable on the day the proceedings were finally concluded.

p32.heading: River Information Services
p32.text: If the Economic Chamber of the Czech Republic appoints a dispatcher, it shall do so on condition that the dispatcher passes an examination of the legal regulations, knowledge of which is necessary for drawing up a dispatch note pursuant to this Act. A person who has passed the examination shall receive a certificate and shall use a stamp with the inscription “Dispatcher of the Economic Chamber of the Czech Republic”.

p32a.text:
(1) The River Information Services system represents a set of information made available free of charge, relating to the support of traffic and transport management in inland navigation on waterways defined in accordance with the relevant European Union regulation^5g) in an implementing legal regulation (hereinafter referred to as “information”). The information maintained in the River Information Services system includes information on the waterway, traffic on the waterway, extraordinary events on the waterway, vessels and transported cargo, and port fees. The administrator of the River Information Services system shall store and make available the information contained therein in electronic form enabling continuous remote access.

(2) The River Information Services system is a public administration information system^5h).

(3) The River Information Services system is divided into a set of publicly accessible information and a set of information with restricted access. The set of information with restricted access includes information on the position of the vessel and information on the cargo on the vessel.

(4) The information items recorded in the River Information Services system and the structure of information with restricted access shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p32b.text:
(1) The administrator of the river information services system shall input into the river information services system information obtained from its own activities and information provided by third parties.

(2) Information relating to the support of traffic and transport management in inland navigation shall be provided to the administrator of the river information services system by:

- a) the operator or master of a vessel registered in the navigation register, the small vessel register, or a similar register pursuant to § 9, if it concerns data on the vessel's position, cargo on the vessel, and extraordinary events on the waterway;

- b) the waterway administrator, if it concerns data on the waterway and vessel movements in navigation locks;

- c) the Czech Hydrometeorological Institute, if it concerns data on climatic conditions on waterways; and

- d) the port operator, if it concerns data on the port relating to its land part, and on vessels in the port.

(3) The structure, form, content, and method of transmitting information to the administrator of the river information services system by the persons listed in paragraph 2 shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p32c.text:
(1) The administrator of the river information services system shall ensure:

- a) continuous receipt of information provided by third parties;

- b) processing of information input into the river information services system in accordance with the requirements for its structure, form, and content, as stipulated by the implementing legal regulation;

- c) continuous input and accessibility of information in the river information services system; and

- d) security of the river information services system against unauthorized access to restricted information, unauthorized alteration, and loss of information.

(2) The administrator of the river information services system shall process and input information into the river information services system without undue delay after becoming aware of it or after verifying it. The administrator of the river information services system is obliged to verify the accuracy of information received from persons not listed in § 32b para. 2.

(3) The administrator of the river information services system shall, based on a written request from the vessel operator, decide on the accessibility of a part of the information set maintained in the river information services system, to which access is restricted. In the request pursuant to the first sentence, the operator must specify the vessels for which they request access to information.

(4) The administrator of the river information services system shall decide on the accessibility of a part of the information set maintained in the river information services system, to which access is restricted, after verifying that the applicant is the operator of the vessels specified in the request pursuant to paragraph 3 in the navigation register, the small vessel register, or a similar register pursuant to § 9. In the decision pursuant to the first sentence, the administrator of the river information services system shall specify the vessels for which restricted information will be made accessible to the applicant.

(5) Immediately after issuing the decision pursuant to paragraph 3, the administrator of the river information services system shall deliver an access code enabling access to the information defined in the decision to the applicant personally.

(6) The operator of a vessel registered in another Member State of the European Union, another contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area, or the Swiss Confederation, for which a decision has been made on the accessibility of a part of the information set maintained in the river information services system pursuant to paragraph 3, is obliged to notify the administrator of the river information services system of a change in the operator of this vessel within 5 days from the date the change occurred.

(7) The administrator of the river information services system shall decide on the termination or restriction of access to a part of the information set maintained in the river information services system and simultaneously terminate or restrict the validity of the access code, if:

- a) there is a change in the entry of the owner or operator in the navigation register, the small vessel register, or a similar register pursuant to § 9 for a vessel for which a decision has been made on the accessibility of a part of the information set maintained in the river information services system, to which access is restricted; or

- b) the vessel operator requests it.

(8) The administrator of the river information services system is obliged to provide data from the information set maintained in the river information services system, to which access is restricted, based on a written request, furthermore to:

- a) state administration authorities;

- b) courts; and

- c) public prosecutor's offices.

(9) The administrator of the river information services system shall make the information set maintained in the river information services system, to which access is restricted, accessible to the Police of the Czech Republic, the Military Police, the Security Information Service, and the competent authority of another Member State of the European Union, another contracting state to the Agreement on the European Economic Area, or the Swiss Confederation operating a river information services system in that state. The administrator of the river information services system shall also make information on the vessel's position accessible to the relevant waterway administrator.

p32d.text: A product intended for use in the river information services system shall be approved by the navigation authority based on a written request, if so stipulated by a directly applicable regulation of the European Union. The navigation authority shall approve the product if the technical requirements for the product stipulated by a directly applicable regulation of the European Union are met. A product for use in the river information services system approved in another Member State of the European Union in accordance with a directly applicable regulation of the European Union shall be considered a product approved under this Act.

p32e.text:
(1) The vessel operator shall equip the vessel with a device and software intended for

- a) monitoring the position and movement of vessels within the inland automatic identification system pursuant to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing technical specifications for vessel tracking and tracing systems^24), and

- b) displaying electronic navigational charts and information on the position and movement of vessels pursuant to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing technical specifications for electronic chart display and information system for inland navigation^25).

(2) The master of the vessel shall use the device and software pursuant to paragraph 1 during navigation; this does not apply to a vessel standing in port. The master of the vessel shall use electronic navigational charts and information on the position and movement of vessels in such a way as to ensure the safety and fluidity of navigation. Should a malfunction occur in the device or software pursuant to paragraph 1 during navigation, the master of the vessel shall immediately report this fact to the administrator of the river information services system. If the master of the vessel makes a report pursuant to the previous sentence, he is entitled to

- a) continue the already commenced voyage for a maximum of 48 hours from the report, and

- b) stand with the vessel in a landing stage or mooring area, the position of which he shall immediately report to the administrator of the river information services system.

(3) Paragraphs 1 and 2 shall apply only to a self-propelled vessel which

- a) is operated on a waterway of significant transport importance used pursuant to paragraph 1 letter a) point 2 or paragraph 1 letter b) point 1 of Annex No. 2 to this Act, and

- b) is

- 1. a ship that is not a recreational craft or a ferry,

- 2. a floating machine, or

- 3. a small vessel with a hull length exceeding 7 meters operated by the waterway administrator, the navigation authority, or a basic component of the integrated rescue system.

(4) In the case of a vessel convoy, paragraphs 1 and 2 shall apply only to the vessel pursuant to paragraph 3 that leads the vessel convoy.

(5) The method of using the device and software pursuant to paragraph 2 shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p33.text:
(1) Water transport for the needs of others may only be operated by a reliable person who holds a concession^8).

(2) A person who has operated water transport for the needs of others unlawfully during the last three years before submitting the application, and a person whose concession for operating water transport was revoked during the last three years before submitting the application due to a breach of obligations imposed by legal regulations related to the operation of water transport, or because they failed to fulfil the conditions stipulated in the concession, shall not be considered reliable for operating water transport for the needs of others.

p33a.heading: Professional competence
p33a.text:
(1) An applicant proves professional competence to operate water transport for the needs of others by a certificate of a successfully passed examination in subjects related to the operation of inland water transport, taken before the navigation authority, and by a document of at least three years of practice in the field of water transport. A more detailed definition of these subjects shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

(2) The certificate of a successfully passed examination pursuant to paragraph 1 shall be replaced by a certificate of a state or matriculation examination in subjects stipulated by the implementing regulation, provided that these subjects were part of it and provided that the navigation authority recognizes this examination from the perspective of professional competence pursuant to this Act.

(3) Professional competence, the content and scope of examinations, and details on taking examinations pursuant to paragraph 1 shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p34.heading: Opinion on concession decision-making
p34.text: In the opinion necessary for the issuance of a concession^8), the navigation authority shall state whether the applicant for the concession meets the condition of reliability (§ 33), the substantive, temporal, and territorial scope of operating water transport, and, if applicable, other conditions for operating water transport.

p35.heading: Rights and obligations of a public water transport operator
p35.text:
