(1) A public water transport operator is obliged to transport a person who requests it for the agreed price, and to accept a consignment for transport for the agreed price,^9) if the contractual transport conditions are met and operational conditions do not prevent it.

(2) Furthermore, before commencing operation, a public water transport operator is obliged to publish in the Commercial Bulletin the contractual transport conditions under which public transport will be operated, the timetable, the tariff, the date of commencement of operation, and the extent of barrier-free access.

p35a.text:
(1) The navigation authority shall, on the basis of a written application from the water transport operator and after verifying the fulfilment of conditions for the transport of goods and passengers on inland waterways of other Member States of the European Union and other contracting states of the Agreement on the European Economic Area in accordance with directly applicable European Union regulations^7a), issue the applicant a certificate of fulfilment of these conditions.

(2) The water transport operator is obliged to notify the navigation authority without undue delay of any change in the conditions, the fulfilment of which was certified pursuant to a directly applicable European Union regulation^18).

(3) The navigation authority shall revoke the certificate of fulfilment of conditions pursuant to paragraph 1 if the conditions for its issuance are not met.

p36.heading: Transport of dangerous goods
p36.text:
(1) Dangerous goods are objects and substances which, due to their nature, properties or condition, may endanger the safety of persons, animals and property or the environment in connection with their transport.

(2) Only dangerous goods defined by an international agreement governing the transport of dangerous goods by inland waterways, which is part of the legal order^19), may be transported on inland waterways, and under the conditions specified therein.

p36a.heading: Safety advisors for the transport of dangerous goods
p36a.text:
(1) Every natural or legal person carrying out activities related to the loading, unloading or transport of dangerous goods by inland waterways is obliged to appoint a safety adviser for the transport of dangerous goods (hereinafter referred to as “safety adviser”).

(2) The main task of the safety adviser is, while maintaining the responsibility of the person pursuant to paragraph 1, to strive by all suitable means and measures that facilitate the transport of dangerous goods in compliance with applicable regulations, for the best possible safety conditions for such transport.

(3) Other tasks of the safety adviser in relation to the person pursuant to paragraph 1 include in particular:

- a) monitoring compliance with regulations for the transport of dangerous goods,

- b) providing recommendations for the transport of dangerous goods,

- c) preparing an annual report on activities in the transport of dangerous goods; these annual reports must be kept for 5 years and must be submitted upon request to the competent state authority,

- d) monitoring procedures for compliance with rules concerning the identification of dangerous goods being transported,

- e) monitoring the acquisition of means of transport for dangerous goods and substances with regard to the specific requirements they must meet,

- f) monitoring the performance of inspections of equipment used in the transport, loading or unloading of dangerous goods,

- g) monitoring whether employees are properly trained, whether records of this training are kept in their file, and whether they have detailed operating procedures and instructions available,

- h) monitoring the application of appropriate emergency procedures in the event of any accident or extraordinary event that may adversely affect the safety of transport, loading or unloading of dangerous goods,

- i) investigating serious accidents, extraordinary events or serious disruptive interventions that occurred during the transport, loading or unloading of dangerous goods, and preparing reports thereon,

- j) applying appropriate measures to prevent the recurrence of accidents, extraordinary events or serious disruptive interventions,

- k) taking into account legal regulations and specific requirements associated with the transport of dangerous goods when selecting and using subcontractors or third parties,

- l) ensuring that accompanying documents and safety equipment are available during transport in accordance with legal regulations.

(4) A safety adviser is a natural person who holds a valid certificate of professional competence as a safety adviser. The condition for obtaining the certificate is the completion of training and passing an examination. The certificate of professional competence as a safety adviser is issued by the navigation authority.

(5) The navigation authority keeps records of applications for the issuance of a certificate of professional competence as a safety adviser and records of natural persons to whom such a certificate has been issued. The processing of personal data is governed by a special legal regulation.^7a)

(6) The navigation authority may, upon request after a selection procedure, grant a legal person with its registered office or organizational unit in the territory of the Czech Republic or a natural person with residence or place of business in the territory of the Czech Republic authorization for activities related to ensuring training and examinations for applicants for the issuance of a certificate of professional competence as a safety adviser. The navigation authority may grant authorization to a person who demonstrates

- a) their independence from safety advisers or persons employing safety advisers,

- b) the ability to ensure equal access of applicants to examinations,

- c) the professional competence of natural persons to conduct training and examinations of applicants.

The authorization is granted for a definite period, but for a maximum of 5 years, and sets out the conditions for the performance of the activity. Before the expiration of the authorization, the navigation authority may, upon request of the holder, extend the validity of the authorization, but for a maximum of 5 years. The navigation authority may revoke the authorization if the holder of the authorization fails to comply with the conditions stipulated therein.

(7) The conditions for the appointment of a safety adviser, the acquisition of a certificate of professional competence as a safety adviser, and the conditions under which other persons may be entrusted with the performance of activities related to ensuring training and examinations for applicants for the issuance of a certificate of professional competence as a safety adviser, shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

(8) The particulars of the application for the grant of authorization for activity pursuant to paragraph 4 and the requirements for the professional knowledge of natural persons who conduct training and examinations of applicants for the issuance of a certificate of professional competence as a safety adviser, shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p36b.heading: Working hours of an employee in water transport
p36b.text:
(1) The water transport operator is obliged to determine the working hours of an employee in water transport so that their length does not exceed

- a) 14 hours during 24 consecutive hours,

- b) 84 hours per week and an average of 72 hours per week over a period of no more than 4 consecutive months, and

- c) 2,304 hours over a period of 12 consecutive months.

(2) If the basic employment relationship was agreed for a period shorter than 12 consecutive months, the maximum length of working hours pursuant to paragraph 1 letter c) shall be proportionally reduced depending on its duration.

(3) The water transport operator is obliged to determine the working hours of an employee in water transport so that their length during night time does not exceed 42 hours per week.

p36c.heading: Rest period and break for food and rest for an employee in water transport
p36c.text:
(1) A water transport operator shall provide a water transport employee with a rest period of at least 10 hours during 24 consecutive hours. The rest period under the first sentence may be divided into two parts, one of which must be at least 6 consecutive hours.

(2) The continuous rest period of a water transport employee may be shortened to 6 hours during 24 consecutive hours if

- a) the safety of the vessel, other vessels, cargo on the vessel, the life or health of water transport employees or other participants in navigation is endangered, and

- b) the subsequent rest period of the water transport employee is extended by the duration of the shortening of this rest period.

(3) Instead of a continuous weekly rest period, the water transport operator shall provide the water transport employee with 1 or more days of rest, depending on the number of consecutive working days immediately preceding their provision. The water transport operator shall provide the water transport employee with at least

- a) 0.2 days of rest for every first to tenth consecutive working day,

- b) 0.3 days of rest for every eleventh to twentieth consecutive working day, and

- c) 0.4 days of rest for every twenty-first to thirty-first consecutive working day.

(4) The water transport operator shall provide the water transport employee with a corresponding number of days of rest no later than after 31 consecutive working days.

(5) If, after exhausting full days of rest, the water transport employee has remaining proportional parts of a day of rest, these proportional parts shall be added up and provided to the water transport employee no later than within 6 months from the day on which the right to a full day of rest arose.

(6) § 88 (1) second and third sentences and § 88 (2) of the Labour Code shall not apply to the provision of breaks for meals and rest to water transport employees. If a break for meals and rest has been divided, each part thereof must be more than 15 minutes.

p36d.heading: Records of working time and rest periods
p36d.text:
(1) Records of working time for a water transport employee shall also include a schedule of working days and rest days, indicating the start and end of each rest day. The records of working time shall further include the name of the vessel master, the identification marks of the vessel on which the water transport employee performs work, and, if it is a ship, its name.

(2) The water transport operator shall submit to the water transport employee, by the end of the month following the month in which the water transport employee performed work for him, an extract from the records of working time signed by the water transport operator or a person authorized by him. The extract from the records of working time shall state the date of its submission to the water transport employee. The water transport employee shall confirm the accuracy of the data contained in the extract from the records of working time by his signature. If the water transport employee refuses to sign the extract, the water transport operator shall note this fact therein. The water transport operator shall hand over one copy of the extract to the water transport employee.

(3) The water transport operator shall keep the extract from the records of working time on the vessel for a period of 1 year from the date of its submission to the employee. The water transport employee shall keep a copy of this extract for a period of 1 year from the date of its receipt.

p36e.heading: Relationship to the Labour Code
p36e.text: Unless this Act provides otherwise, labour law relations between a water transport operator and a water transport employee shall be governed by the Labour Code.

p37.heading: International transport
p37.text:
(1) For the operation of water transport in or through the territory of the Czech Republic by a foreign operator, a permit from the navigation authority is required, unless an international treaty by which the Czech Republic is bound provides otherwise. A foreign water transport operator is a natural person with permanent residence or a legal entity with its registered office outside the territory of the Czech Republic, who operates water transport with vessels registered in the navigation register of a foreign state. A national of a Member State of the European Union, another contracting State to the Agreement on the European Economic Area or the Swiss Confederation, or a legal entity with its registered office in one of these States, who are authorized to operate water transport in another Member State of the European Union, in another contracting State to the Agreement on the European Economic Area or in the Swiss Confederation and who operate water transport with vessels registered in the navigation register of one of these States, shall not be considered a foreign water transport operator.

(2) A permit may be issued to a foreign water transport operator who is authorized to operate water transport according to the regulations valid in the state where he has his registered office or residence. A permit shall not be issued if the requested transport is contrary to the interests of the Czech Republic.

p37a.text:
(1) The Ministry of Transport

- a) monitors the development of the supply of vessel capacity intended for the transport of goods in water transport registered in the navigation register,

- b) evaluates the intensity of water transport in relation to vessel capacity, throughput, and the condition of waterways,

- c) monitors whether the development of vessel capacity supply and the intensity of water transport does not lead to a serious disruption of the financial management of water transport operators.

(2) Water transport operators shall provide the Ministry of Transport, in January and July of each year, with a statement of overall financial results for the previous period in connection with the operation of water transport.

p37b.text:
The provisions of § 37a do not apply to the capacity of vessels

- a) operated exclusively on a waterway not connected to the waterways of another Member State of the European Union,

- b) intended for pushing with propulsion up to 300 kW,

- c) which are operated for the purpose of non-profit public transport,

- d) whose carrying capacity is less than 450 tons,

- e) which have the character of a floating machine or floating equipment or a floating body, or

- f) of the waterway administrator.

p37c.text:
A market disturbance in water transport occurs if

- a) the development of vessel capacity supply exceeds the development of demand for at least six consecutive months, or

- b) the intensity of water transport causes long-term impassability of waterways, for at least six months,

and if, for these reasons, a significant number of water transport operators are threatened with insolvency according to a special legal regulation.^8a)

p37d.text:
(1) If a market disturbance in water transport occurs, the Ministry of Transport shall request the Commission to issue a decision on the regulation of vessel capacity.^8b) For this purpose, it shall provide the Commission with documents and information justifying the request, in particular average costs and prices of various types of transport, rates for the use of vessel space, and demand estimates.

(2) The Ministry of Transport shall publish the Commission's decision on the regulation of vessel capacity in the Transport Bulletin.

p37e.text:
(1) If a market disturbance in water transport occurs, the Ministry of Transport shall establish a water transport account (hereinafter referred to as the „account“) for the purpose of its regulation. Water transport operators contribute to the water transport account under conditions stipulated by a special regulation.^8b) The funds in the account are other state financial resources and are earmarked. When managing the income and expenditure of the account and its administration, the Ministry of Transport shall also be guided by European Union regulations.^8c)

(2) The Minister of Transport shall decide on the use of funds from the account under the conditions and within the limits stipulated in the Commission's decision. The Administrative Procedure Code shall not apply to the decision of the Minister of Transport on the use of funds from the account. The decision of the Minister of Transport shall be published in the Transport Bulletin.

(3) Unspent funds in the account shall be transferred to the following calendar year at the end of the calendar year.

p37f.text:
(1) The Ministry of Transport shall discuss the report on the management of account funds for the relevant calendar year with the relevant transport associations representing water transport operators and shall publish it in the Transport Bulletin.

(2) A water transport operator has the right to inspect data on the management of account funds and to request an explanation from the Ministry of Transport if they have justified doubts about the method of accounting for their relationship to the account.

(3) Details on the keeping of the account and the method of allocating financial resources from the account shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p38.text:
(1) The administrative authority State Navigation Administration, with its registered office in Prague, is hereby established and is subordinate to the Ministry of Transport.

(2) The head of the State Navigation Administration is the director; his selection, appointment, and dismissal are governed by the State Service Act.

p39.text: State administration according to this Act is performed by navigation authorities, which are the Ministry of Transport and the State Navigation Administration.

p40.text:
(1) The State Navigation Administration performs its competence under this Act, with the exception of matters in which the Ministry of Transport decides.

(2) The State Navigation Administration is the administrator of the river information services system.

(3) The State Navigation Administration further

- a) controls the fulfillment of obligations concerning the observance of passenger rights according to directly applicable European Union regulation^20) and handles complaints about the violation of these obligations,

- b) performs the activities of the competent authority according to the international agreement regulating the transport of dangerous goods by inland waterways, which is part of the legal order^19),

- c) ensures the transmission of information about the facts stated in § 29b par. 1 letter h) to the integrated rescue system authorities,

- d) carries out prevention in the field of navigation safety.

(4) If the obligations stipulated in paragraph 3 letter a) concern a travel agency operator or a travel agent operator, the municipal trade licensing office supervises their fulfillment and handles complaints about their violation.

(5) The State Navigation Administration shall ensure, for the purpose of proper performance of state administration, including timely detection of deficiencies and their remedy, the introduction of a quality management system in accordance with the harmonized Czech technical standard regulating quality management^31).

p41.text:
The Ministry of Transport

- a) authorizes a legal entity to carry out technical inspections of vessels,

- b) decides on the approval of the type of product intended for use in the river information services system,

- c) decides on the authorization of persons to conduct training intended for a vessel crew member or for the performance of the activity of a passenger transport expert and on the withdrawal of such authorization,

- d) conducts an examination of theoretical knowledge and practical skills necessary for conducting training intended for a vessel crew member or for the performance of the activity of a passenger transport expert and appoints and dismisses members of the examination committee before which this examination is taken,

- e) decides on the approval of technical suitability, on technical unsuitability, and on the prohibition of using a simulator to verify the practical skills of a vessel master or practical skills necessary for navigating a vessel using radar, notifies the Commission and affected international organizations operating in the field of inland navigation of data on these decisions, and establishes an expert committee to carry out a technical inspection of the simulator,

- f) issues an opinion for decisions on concessions for operating water transport,

- g) decides on granting permission for operating international water transport,

- h) is the appellate body in administrative proceedings in matters regulated by this Act against decisions of the State Navigation Administration,

- i) submits to the Commission a request for recognition of a classification society or its cancellation according to the European Union regulation governing technical requirements for inland navigation vessels^5a),

- j) carries out prevention in the field of navigation safety.

p42.heading: State supervision
p42.text:
(1) State supervision of waterways and traffic thereon shall be exercised by the Ministry of Transport and the State Navigation Authority.

(2) Authorization to exercise state supervision shall be issued by the administrative authority within whose competence the exercise of supervision falls. The authorization of employees of the State Navigation Authority to exercise state supervision shall be in the form of an identity card.

(3) In exercising state supervision, an employee of the navigation authority authorized to exercise state supervision (hereinafter referred to as the „inspecting person“) shall supervise whether administrators of monitored waterways, operators of ports, landing stages, transhipment points, mooring points and anchorages, vessel operators, operators of small vessel rental services, water transport operators, participants in navigation traffic and other persons fulfil the obligations stipulated by this Act.

(4) If the inspecting person discovers a breach of obligations stipulated by this Act during the exercise of state supervision, they shall, as necessary and according to the nature of the deficiencies found, specify the manner and deadline for rectifying these deficiencies and their causes.

(5) The inspecting person shall prohibit the navigation of a vessel if they find that

- a) the vessel does not have a valid vessel certificate or special navigation permit,

- b) the maximum load of passengers or cargo on the vessel is exceeded,

- c) the vessel does not meet the conditions of technical fitness and clearly endangers the safety of persons on board, the environment, or the safety of navigation traffic,

- d) the vessel does not meet the crew manning requirements stipulated by this Act, or

- e) the inspected person performs the function of a crew member or the activity of an expert in passenger transport or an expert in liquefied natural gas, operates a floating machine, or operates a small or recreational vessel under the influence of alcohol or another addictive substance.

(6) If the navigation of a vessel has been prohibited pursuant to paragraph 5 letter c), the inspecting person shall also retain the vessel certificate issued or whose validity was extended by the navigation authority, until the conditions of technical fitness of the vessel are met again. The inspecting person may issue a provisional vessel certificate to the owner or operator of a vessel whose navigation has been prohibited pursuant to paragraph 5 letter c), for the purpose of navigating to the place of repair or inspection of the vessel, provided that its navigability is reasonably ensured. The provisional vessel certificate shall cease to be valid no later than 30 days from its issuance. The renewed fulfilment of technical fitness conditions shall be verified by the navigation authority through a technical inspection, to which § 10 paragraphs 3 and 4 shall apply mutatis mutandis. If the vessel continues not to meet the technical fitness conditions, the navigation authority shall decide on its technical unfitness and revoke the vessel certificate. An appeal against the decision on the technical unfitness of the vessel shall not have suspensive effect. The vessel certificate shall cease to be valid upon the decision on the technical unfitness of the vessel.

(7) If the navigation authority finds that a vessel does not meet the technical fitness conditions or if its navigation has been prohibited pursuant to paragraph 5 letter c), it shall inform the authority of the European Union Member State that issued or extended the validity of the vessel certificate of this fact within 7 days.

(8) The particulars of the identity card of employees of the State Navigation Authority, its specimen, the procedure for carrying out a technical inspection following the exercise of state supervision, and its scope according to individual types of vessels or the technical fitness conditions stipulated for them shall be laid down by an implementing legal regulation.

p43.heading: Offences of natural persons

p44.heading: Administrative Offenses Committed by Legal Entities and Natural Persons Carrying out Business Activities

p45.text:
(1) Misdemeanours under this Act shall be heard by

- a) the State Navigation Authority, or

- b) the municipal trade licensing office, in the case of misdemeanours under § 44 (12) or (13), if the perpetrator is a tour operator or travel agency operator.

(2) Fines imposed by the State Navigation Authority shall be collected and enforced by the customs office.

p47.text: The provisions of § 9 to 21 and § 24 to 27 shall apply to vessels of the Army of the Czech Republic and vessels of the Police of the Czech Republic, their masters and crew members, unless a special regulation provides otherwise.

p48.text: The provisions of Decree of the Ministry of Transport No. 65/1967 Sb., on boatmen's and seamen's books, shall not apply insofar as they concern inland navigation.

p48b.text: In proceedings for the issuance of a certificate of competence, the navigation authority shall obtain a digitized photograph of the applicant or a digital processing of their likeness or photograph and a digitized handwritten signature of the applicant for the purpose of its issuance. If any of these data cannot be obtained from the register of identity cards, the register of travel documents, or the agenda information system for foreigners, the navigation authority shall acquire it; the navigation authority shall proceed analogously if there has been a substantial change in the applicant's likeness.

p49.heading: Decision-making of the navigation authority on exemptions from the Act
p49.text:
(1) The navigation authority shall, upon request of the vessel operator, grant an exemption from the navigation prohibition under § 22 (1) (c) and (d) and (3), if the safety and fluidity of navigation traffic are not thereby endangered.

(2) The navigation authority shall, upon request, grant an exemption from the prohibition of sailing on a float with a sail or using a traction kite as stipulated in § 29e (2), if the safety and fluidity of navigation traffic are not thereby endangered.

(3) If it is necessary to ensure the safety and fluidity of navigation traffic, the navigation authority shall, by a measure of a general nature, grant for a period strictly necessary for a certain section of the waterway an exemption from the maximum permitted values of vessel dimensions and vessel configurations, from the maximum permitted values of vessel draught, and from the maximum permitted navigation speed stipulated on the basis of § 29k. The navigation authority shall publish the measure of a general nature after its entry into force through the river information services system. The waterway administrator shall mark the defined part of the waterway with navigation signs for the period stipulated by the measure of a general nature.

p51.heading: Transitional provisions
p51.text:
(1) The rights and obligations of the budgetary organization "State Navigation Authority" established by the Ministry of Transport existing on the date of its abolition shall pass to the State Navigation Authority established under this Act on the date of entry into force of this Act.

(2) A vessel operator who may operate a vessel on a waterway only on condition of liability insurance for damage from vessel operation concluded by its operator, is obliged to conclude this insurance within two years from the effective date of this Act. Until the conclusion of this insurance, but no longer than two years from the effective date of this Act, they shall be considered a vessel operator who meets the insurance condition under this Act.

(3) Ship certificates and special permits for the operation of floating equipment issued under regulations valid before the effective date of this Act shall be considered vessel certificates under this Act.

(4) Certificates of competence for vessel masters and vessel crew members issued under regulations valid before the effective date of this Act shall remain valid.

p53.heading: Repealing provisions
p53.text:
The following are hereby repealed:

- 1. Act No. 26/1964 Sb., on inland navigation, as amended by Act No. 126/1974 Sb.,

- 2. Decree of the Federal Ministry of Transport No. 137/1974 Sb., on inland navigation, as amended by Decree of the Federal Ministry of Transport No. 94/1976 Sb.,

- 3. Government Regulation of the Czechoslovak Socialist Republic No. 11/1976 Sb., on the registration of inland vessels,

- 4. Decree of the Ministry of Transport No. 65/1964 Sb., on port regulations for Czechoslovak ports,

- 5. Decree of the Ministry of Transport No. 66/1964 Sb., on transshipment and storage regulations for ports designated for public use,

- 6. Decree of the Ministry of Transport No. 134/1964 Sb., on water transport regulations, as amended by Decree of the Federal Ministry of Transport No. 31/1982 Sb. and Decree of the Federal Ministry of Transport No. 23/1984 Sb.,

- 7. Edict of the Federal Ministry of Transport Ref. No. 15 000/1975-25, which designates technical equipment on vessels and in ports serving the direct security of water transport operations, and stipulates the method of exercising supervision over such designated technical equipment (registered in Part 27/1975 Sb.),

- 8. Edict of the Federal Ministry of Transport Ref. No. 26 357/1975 on the professional competence of inland vessel crew members (registered in Part 39/1975 Sb.),

- 9. Edict of the Federal Ministry of Transport Ref. No. 22 690/1983-025 on equipping recreational vessels sailing under the international navigation regime with ship documents (registered in Part 20/1984 Sb.),

- 10. Edict of the Federal Ministry of Transport Ref. No. 9737/1984-025 on the transport of military container shipments (registered in Part 22/1984 Sb.),

- 11. Edict of the Federal Ministry of Transport Ref. No. 19 486/1985-25 on the issuance of Authorization for the exercise of state expert supervision over inland navigation (registered in Part 33/1985 Sb.).

p54.text:
This Act shall enter into force on 1 October 1995.

sprilohac1.text:
Components of a waterway

The components of a waterway are:

- 1. bank fortifications, navigation bridges, navigation tunnels, and boat passes,

- 2. coastal service roads of the watercourse, navigation markings on the water surface, on the banks, on bridges, and on other objects and facilities that cross waterways,

- 3. closing structures on navigation canals, water level regulation devices, reservoirs for improving flows on the waterway, water gauges, and warning devices,

- 4. weirs and other impounding structures,

- 5. navigation locks, boat lifts (vertical or inclined) including approaches and saving basins, or other special navigation devices for navigation control and safety,

- 6. port basins, bank modifications, quay walls, and mooring facilities of ports,

- 7. mooring and guiding devices (mooring buoys, dolphins, fender piles, navigation beacons, guardrails, and fender beams),

- 8. movable gangways and other facilities of landing stages,

- 9. devices for collecting navigation fees,

- 10. control rooms and other facilities and objects serving directly the operation of the waterway or its components,

- 11. special service facilities designated for supplying vessels with fuel and pumping out wastewater from vessels.

sprilohac2.text:
List of waterways significant for transport

- 1. Waterways in use are:

- a) the Labe river

- 1. from river km 973.5 (Kunětice) to river km 951.2 (Přelouč lock headwater),

- 2. from river km 949.1 (2.080 km from the axis of the Přelouč weir) to river km 726.6 (state border with the Federal Republic of Germany), including the navigation channel delimited by navigation marks on the Velké Žernoseky water area,

- b) the Vltava river

- 1. from river km 91.5 (Třebenice) to the confluence with the Labe river, including the outlet section of the Berounka river to Radotín port,

- 2. from river km 241.4 (České Budějovice) to river km 91.5 (Třebenice), including the outlet section of the Malše river to river km 1.6, only for vessels with a carrying capacity of up to 300 tons,

- c) the Morava river from the mouth of the Bečva river to the confluence with the Dyje river, including the Otrokovice – Rohatec canal (Baťa Canal).

- 2. Usable waterways are:

- a) the Labe river from river km 987.8 (Opatovice) to river km 973.5 (Kunětice) and from river km 951.2 (Přelouč lock headwater) to river km 949.1 (2.080 km from the axis of the Přelouč weir),

- b) the Bečva river from Přerov to the mouth of the Morava river,

- c) the Odra river from Polanka nad Odrou to the state border with Poland,

- d) the Ostravice river below the mouth of the Lučina,

- e) the Ohře river from river km 3.0 (Terezín) to its mouth into the Labe river.
